name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 7/13/2025
body:
What does the future hold for Colorado's children?
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**July 13, 2025** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST**
| | Dear Readers,
**What kind of Colorado are we leaving for our kids?**
This week’s headlines are a sobering reminder that our children are at the center of a cultural and political storm.

In Jefferson County, schools are under fire after an alarming report was released by Jeffco Kids First, a grassroots advocacy group led by teacher turned activist Lindsay Datko, revealing [33 cases of staff misconduct](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), including sexual abuse and grooming. Some offenders were quietly reassigned. Others resigned with no state report filed.
And at the same time, Colorado schools are facing a [$70 million funding freeze](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) over civil rights complaints tied to DEI and gender policies. In the small town of Paonia, Colorado (pop. 1,500), a local nonprofit is drawing controversy for hosting a [“Sex Health Education Day Camp”](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) for kids ages 10 to 13, set for July 14–16.
But one family is feeling hope after years of being in the trenches, fighting the gender ideology battle here in Colorado.

In Boulder Valley School District, [BJ and Brecken Jones were branded “hateful”](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) for trying to opt their six-year-old daughter out of a transgender-themed lesson they felt clashed with their faith. Their quiet request was denied. Their private records were leaked. And their family was publicly shamed at a school board meeting flooded with activists. But on June 27, the U.S. Supreme Court ruled that parents do have the right to opt out of LGBTQ+ instruction on religious grounds—vindicating the Joneses and families like them nationwide.
These aren’t isolated stories. They are warnings. Our schools are shaping the next generation—and too often, they’re doing it behind closed doors and at the expense of both truth and parental rights.
It’s a perfect storm of neglect: ideology over accountability, activism over safety, and government over parents.
At Rocky Mountain Voice, we believe parents are not the problem—they are the solution. That’s why we’ll keep reporting what others ignore and giving voice to those the system tries to silence.
**Because the future of Colorado’s kids depends on it.**
In liberty and truth,
Team RMV
---
|
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[Polis Plans Special Session After Trump’s Budget Bombshell: Dems See Opportunity to Revive Agenda](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE SUM & SUBSTANCE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Passage Thursday of the budget-cutting “One Big Beautiful Bill” essentially mandates that the Legislature will return to the Capitol for a special session this summer to deal with aspects of the federal law impacting Medicaid funding and artificial-intelligence regulation.

**[“They called us hateful”: Colorado parents see their story reflected in Supreme Court ruling](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) When the U.S. Supreme Court ruled that public schools must allow parents to opt their children out of LGBTQ+ instruction that conflicts with their religious beliefs, BJ and Brecken Jones sat quietly in their living room—stunned, relieved and vindicated.
__
**[ICE expansion in Colorado draws fire as private prison firms compete for contracts](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[CBS COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Just days after the passage of President Trump’s budget bill allocating $45 billion to Immigration and Customs Enforcement to expand detention facilities, the American Civil Liberties Union of Colorado made public documents submitted by private prison operators seeking to sign contracts with the federal government.

|
---
**STATE**
**[DEI at a cost: Colorado schools caught in $70M federal freeze](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Colorado public schools are facing a $70 million funding freeze—part of a broader $6.8 billion nationwide hold triggered by the U.S. Department of Education. At the heart of the conflict are federal objections to DEI and LGBTQIA+ initiatives in schools, which have already led to lawsuits and pushed districts to search for new funding before fall.

**[Ranchers Beg for Relief as Colorado Wolf Attacks Mount](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE COLORADO SUN](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Only a few ranchers were expected to come to the meeting held in Chris Collins’ shop on the McCabe Ranch in Old Snowmass, which smelled of the smoked venison sausages cooking on the grill, horses on jeans, and a mixture of sweat and anxiety.

**[Parents Push Back: Colorado’s Gender Policies Trigger Rights Showdown](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[COLORADO POLITICS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Amid the staccato of legislation that Democrats at the Colorado Capitol adopted to expand what sponsors described as transgender “rights,” a growing number of parents are pushing back, arguing that the state government has overreached, impeding their ability to raise their children the way they see fit.

_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[They never saw it coming: Kerrville flood sparks call for weather readiness](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) At least 82 people lost their lives in Kerrville when floodwaters tore through the area in the middle of the night. Families had no warning. Rain poured harder and faster than forecasted. In Kerrville, the flood was already doing damage before most people knew it was coming.
__
**[Hearing lays groundwork for GOP-led reform after 3-year immigration surge, 500K visa overstays](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The U.S. House Judiciary Committee held a hearing June 25 on visa security and legal immigration pathways, revisiting the Trump administration’s policies and examining how current enforcement gaps could be putting national security at risk.

**[HHS shuts door on illegal immigrants’ access to taxpayer-funded benefits](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[JUST THE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The Department of Health and Human Services on Thursday banned illegal immigrants from accessing the agency’s taxpayer-funded benefits. Since 1998, HHS has interpreted the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996 to let illegal immigrants use certain federal public benefits, according to the department.
**[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[Jeffco Kids First report reveals 33 staff misconduct cases in Jefferson County Schools](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) An alarming report released by Jeffco Kids First, a grassroots advocacy group led by teacher turned activist Lindsay Datko, has exposed 33 cases of sexual abuse and misconduct by staff in Jefferson County Public Schools since 2022. The article “33 Documented Sexual Abuse and Misconduct Cases in Jeffco Schools” presents a disturbing image of a district with systemic failures.
**[Boll: A day camp in Paonia shows how far the agenda to sexualize kids has gone](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _In the small, conservative town of Paonia, Colorado, with a population of just 1,500, a local organization is stirring debate with a new initiative aimed at children. The Learning Council, a tax-exempt nonprofit based in downtown Paonia, is hosting a “Sex Health Education Day Camp” for 10- to 13-year-olds on July 14–16._
**[Baker: Freedom still rings loud in Brush](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _In Northeast Colorado, where faith, family, and freedom still shape daily life, over 1,200 residents lined the streets under clear skies to celebrate the 70th Annual Brush Fourth of July Parade. This wasn’t just another parade—it was a full-hearted tribute to the traditions and people who keep the American spirit alive._
**_[READ MORE LOCAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_**
|
---
**COMMENTARY**
**[Ganahl: Trump’s new MABA commission puts conservation back in the hands of the people](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _While most folks were watching fireworks, President Trump was signing something far more lasting—an Executive Order that could reshape how we care for the land we love. It’s the kind of bold step we need to bring common sense back to how we manage the wild, wide-open spaces that make this country worth fighting for._ __
**[Garbo: The Hippocratic Oath demands compassion—even for MAGA voters suffering from Texas floods](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _The moment Dr. Christina B. Propst chose to mock flood-stricken Texans as “getting what they voted for,” she exposed a depth of moral bankruptcy that defies belief. In a now-deleted Facebook post under the name “Chris Tina,” she wished safety only for “children, non-MAGA voters and pets,” while implying that political affiliation should determine who deserves compassion amid a deadly disaster._
__
**[O’Reilly and Minary: What Douglas County’s Home Rule vote revealed—and how we move forward](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Voters in Douglas County turned down the proposed Home Rule initiative by a wide margin—71% to 29%. But the result wasn’t simply a rejection of the idea itself. It also reflected deeper concerns about how the process unfolded and how residents were—or weren’t—brought into the conversation._
__
**[Pastor Bob: “Shhhhh Pastor–you can’t say that!”](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _“Shhhhh, Pastor, you can’t say that!” That’s what they want us to believe. That if we dare speak the truth about what’s going on in our country, we’re risking our church’s tax status. But here’s the thing—truth doesn’t come with a mute button._ __
**[Devotional: The compass that always points to truth](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _I’ll never forget the sting of the Texas sun at Camp Bullis, just outside of San Antonio. It was the next phase of my training, following Basic Training and the Police Academy at Lackland Air Force Base. This training wasn’t just about learning the ins and outs of the M60 machine gun or polishing our field discipline—it was survival training in its rawest form. Out there in the wild terrain, you either adapted or you got lost. Literally._
**[READ MORE COMMENTARY](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|

|
---
**🥦 DID YOU KNOW?** McDonald’s once tried to make **broccoli taste like bubblegum**. The goal was to get kids to eat healthier—but the experiment flopped when child testers were baffled by the flavor and found it downright confusing.

**QUOTE OF THE DAY** "The most urgent necessity is, not that the State should teach, but that it should allow education. All monopolies are detestable, but the worst of all is the monopoly of education." – Frédéric Bastiat

Peter,

_One year ago today, they tried to silence President Trump --**for good.**_

The fake impeachments weren't enough. Neither were the bogus indictments.

**On July 13th, 2024, in Butler, Pennsylvania, a bullet nearly ended the life of the 45th and soon-to-be 47th President of the United States.**

Let that sink in.

They didn't just come after President Trump.

_They came after all of us who believed in the America First Movement. They came after our voice. Our vote. Our values._

**But here 's the truth, they'll never understand...you can't stop a movement built on freedom. You can't silence millions of proud patriots. And you sure as hell can't stop Donald J. Trump.**

[](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=783bbbe8afef4ddbb4760699f16e92e9&_e=yAsGRF8yTgvlEFbtXjWgASwlFkJHLBiUQN9sXrLOhSFV1jwryB2TsyJ7YcQX_ShPYcvACfew-Bs6d4fYTU0IdbK4PLrG2f8XLwo36OvprlbU-BNDWRlsxbh96IrLhJym4oVaILQd56tTqRXISiPtzwybfVZI8bhsG8ukoMifFxejwLvT31k8Gp0gRRdTctOMBuj-7ZttBH1rLRV6KySO8OGYzg3BlbIGPHaftYs_zpkolTAUOvIyx2HNW40ROFISh57SqEw34NKHKXpdXc8wjwisYkDz2mXY6IOgwa6i2Lv1lc8wvX6foeWxGWOi7Rgp5DvpNRJETHJXmqr9MYjnWMs8NQtPJAshw7i8IN1yepAW8zO3dgl0yAl1_YgmPfR31-ZewMDNUuSFqJ6Ti49v5GeknnE6LJsseF0oOe-9JgAsFAA2MnlLVkV20oUd0PTa-rgRwuK9g5F6LttydYDrD2srob2lFJyhs0W8rIWcDWsTtTfkcRdoL29uecD950sxiuz3e-15-XVbNYKRaeavBicbOpS07p6zod3mjASIC7s%3D)
So today, on the anniversary of the day they crossed the line, we FIGHT.

**[We’re calling every single patriot in America to rally behind President Trump and our America First Movement. Together, we will show them that we will NEVER SURRENDER.](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=783bbbe8afef4ddbb4760699f16e92e9&_e=yAsGRF8yTgvlEFbtXjWgASwlFkJHLBiUQN9sXrLOhSFV1jwryB2TsyJ7YcQX_ShPYcvACfew-Bs6d4fYTU0IdbK4PLrG2f8XLwo36OvprlbU-BNDWRlsxbh96IrLhJym4oVaILQd56tTqRXISiPtz4eyYR4x08HaUlWwTPEPuKNk8fbRBxf4bFhGTNRDBLhjQ1ecWjb9BZ98LfIhZDKKVa98aZBnbhr_j9UCZJ7zXuwsYUi-WeDyFGWvchVA9-2CacQprxmfI04dj4akJFCXY8i7UQpyGnyHSJ2JAojZghQNXU0TJ9jWwOAru-UBpFQ41a27uPs5AzcLfLARouR9REDGpQP3dd90AVBpnMWnXzEQpmSCKLaPqIH-PfTITutbT_dMbVRxwwsVyyLxvyfyW1jn_OXh2Nl0I9tH-gQPw3Dfhbb5ldCUNqSTzn72BDidBydEevH-ub4im5_oVg57hFmZSnpjswbR6RzLpvl5ei3-gZgAsTPazR4EjGN_NG5aw__ZM04nMGQNjhosR6oKP-whRhRjPv2SWS6_GixOsjYTPxkugFEUeSHv1-cbxNLt)**

**It 's up to us to FIGHT BACK, FIGHT, PROUD, AND FIGHT FOR MAGA.**

For America,
Coach Tommy Tuberville

| [](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=783bbbe8afef4ddbb4760699f16e92e9&_e=yAsGRF8yTgvlEFbtXjWgASwlFkJHLBiUQN9sXrLOhSFV1jwryB2TsyJ7YcQX_ShPYcvACfew-Bs6d4fYTU0IdbK4PLrG2f8XLwo36OvprlbU-BNDWRlsxbh96IrLhJym4oVaILQd56tTqRXISiPtz4eyYR4x08HaUlWwTPEPuKNk8fbRBxf4bFhGTNRDBLhjoUr7FHae39Z3ZAlw7dCN_D2Nii-pL5Kyz4jmurVVVgr4h7fYyNTKSE9pZWrPa2A997pVd8dp6A5iMIvgcqtXsl7zFCIpDPKpzDq8SJ7jyySn8d2j9CnBdoNAxgLCmAK1ONTUf-SH9wZIKmRPUstf1J7Xfl3DfI0Id7BCusQIf0mIHerV2nKcoOCtYbGUlpNrq40Us6OhUVxttPVMVG_tFutIvXGGZSs8Ix5r3DLbklQrGlr0DyU8ZJWe1VQ6-6kZFz2vWRPc32aLnXn-pFF1WL3MH1Q5l6AYgr6ZAa7y5pvarKc1Dy6eLJaWIMrkCVew8Ek_LgwNMktxji9H0ZR0yGevxv_0CLnYGNM-o1tc0TJtpKVeet1PP6ZxjsHGiba6)

Meet Coach Tuberville:

**Coach Tuberville was born in Camden, Arkansas, the third of three children of Olive and Charles Tuberville. College Football 's 2004 Coach of the Year and two-time SEC Coach of the Year, Tommy Tuberville is most often recognized for his success on the field. But it is the legacy of his father, a highly decorated WWII veteran and Purple Heart recipient, that motivates Coach Tuberville to give back to the brave men and women of the United States Armed Forces.

In 1989 Coach Tuberville met his future wife, Suzanne Fette, at the Sugar Bowl in New Orleans. They were married in 1991 and have two boys, Tucker and Troy, who continue to make their dad proud.

In his career as a College Football Coach, Tommy started a long and successful career at the University of Miami, starting as a graduate assistant and rising to the Hurricanes' defensive coordinator. He led the Canes to three AP national championships and coached future Hall of Famers Ray Lewis and Warren Sapp, as well as Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson.

In 1998, Coach Tuberville was announced as the next head coach for the Auburn Tigers. Tuberville's time at Auburn was a success by any measure, as the Tigers captured eight consecutive bowl appearances, including five New Year's Day bowl appearances, five SEC Western Division titles, one SEC Championship, and a 13-0 season in 2004, after which Coach Tuberville was once again named the AP's SEC Coach of the Year. For his efforts during the perfect 2004 season, Tuberville would be named Paul "Bear" Bryant Coach of the Year, Associated Press Coach of the Year, Walter Camp Coach of the Year, Sporting News Coach of the Year, and AFCA Coach of the Year, among others.

_In 2020, Coach Tuberville became Senator Tuberville, winning his election to represent all Alabamians in the United States Senate with the full support of President Trump. Since then, he's supported President Trump's America-First policies while fighting off the Radical Left. In his first term as Senator, Tommy was one of eight Republicans to vote against certifying the 2020 election, has been a strong pro-life advocate, and called out Democrats for being pro-crime.

Now, Coach Tuberville is running for Governor so he can continue representing Alabamians and Americans in his homestate of Alabama. The Radical Left thinks they can flip Alabama blue and turn it into the California of the South. Can he count on your support? _

** [**HELP COACH TUBERVILLE**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=783bbbe8afef4ddbb4760699f16e92e9&_e=yAsGRF8yTgvlEFbtXjWgASwlFkJHLBiUQN9sXrLOhSFV1jwryB2TsyJ7YcQX_ShPYcvACfew-Bs6d4fYTU0IdbK4PLrG2f8XLwo36OvprlbU-BNDWRlsxbh96IrLhJym4oVaILQd56tTqRXISiPtz4eyYR4x08HaUlWwTPEPuKNk8fbRBxf4bFhGTNRDBLhjRCFlHrd1e0Qovj-d-JWTF6YNrV9dNq_DJJjPkQQEvAWar7gTZ-aL0vbvqFUklxMo9Z19nh0umyPNq8J-XPdXdU2LRZZ3c1_ndteurQD07vGNygspCyob3GkOLZmR1OEn_hOWaZwe2VnTuo314HMTMNzkX31ifdJf8RUJboua7p4wmhV4cG7vVvnokG_WxwMo4ILOtXfEYNre346Ai51GQrbhcSQn7xG9cQC3uL33TZ-oRjGuPnf5sdl_B7grrz1Fy1HIJFV8ant_fNQpyY6AyuWoljMCdvARwzQ1LtRPZqdwh7KzS0nvLPYwl0SY9g7C4coLDkPj9duJzMIjfdpq2EsFddrA07DChm6KjsEuHyuzeRgymWeKJviWXivw82ED)

name: Phil Weiser
subject: Thank you for signing up!
body:
Dear Friend,

Thanks for signing up to receive email updates from me and my campaign. Whether you are a long time supporter or someone new to my work and curious to learn more, I am glad to have you in the loop.

In this day and age, I know that many of us are flooded with emails and texts from political campaigns. That's why I want to be transparent about the kinds of messages you will receive from me and my team.

Every week, I sit down to write a newsletter that I send to you on Mondays. My campaign and my work as a public servant is informed by hearing from you; your feedback is not only invaluable, it is essential. I look forward to your engagement as I share updates on my work as your AG, and learn more about my priorities in my campaign to be your next Governor.

Our democracy is a team sport–and I really appreciate you being on my team.

Phil

[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/81362/images/Phil%20Weiser.png]

disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
Senate Dems need you in this fight!
| **FIRST: Bernie Sanders launched his Fighting Oligarchy tour!

THEN: Cory Booker made HISTORY with his 25 hour speech on the Senate floor!

_NOW: Adam Schiff is SLAMMING Trump and his MAGA mob for putting billionaires over the American people! ⬇️_** [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/91874?t=6&akid=20545%2E878179%2EeppugC) [**We need 2,000 supporters reading this to join the fight and THANK Senate Democrats like Sanders, Booker, and Schiff for always having our backs! You’ve been hand-selected »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/91875?t=7&akid=20545%2E878179%2EeppugC) | [**THANK SENATE DEMOCRATS: DONATE $3 TO SIGN »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/91875?t=8&akid=20545%2E878179%2EeppugC)
---
TO: Senate Democrats
FROM: A proud supporter
Subject: THANK YOU! Dear Senate Democrats,

**THANK YOU for leading the charge against Donald Trump and his MAGA minions as they attack our democracy!**

I know that Trump has put a huge target on your backs — so it means the world that you aren’t backing down even a little bit.

**_Our work is far from over:_ Republicans are plotting to END Social Security and Medicaid, DESTROY the federal government, and make Trump an untouchable KING.**

As a proud Democrat, you have my word that I will join your fight and do _everything_ I can to help take back the Senate and stop this dangerous administration.

[**So I'm signing this letter and donating $3 to the Voter Protection Project to show that I stand with you, and to THANK YOU for putting it all on the line to save our country! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/91875?t=9&akid=20545%2E878179%2EeppugC) | [**RUSH $3 TO SIGN ON BEFORE WE CLOSE THIS LETTER AT MIDNIGHT »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/91875?t=10&akid=20545%2E878179%2EeppugC)
---
**_X A proud Democrat_**
\- The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=20545%2E878179%2EeppugC). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=20545%2E878179%2EeppugC).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=20545%2E878179%2EeppugC) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=11&akid=20545%2E878179%2EeppugC). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=20545%2E878179%2EeppugC)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=20545%2E878179%2EeppugC)

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111430225/557224704/-74077192?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.07.13_Conor-OCallaghan-Endorsement_B1_F1_S1_C1_AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYwODgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMjBkYWU0ZWItNGM2MC1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=c9PxmuBgB19ybgOLrNg5jb8NWQJtGuKn4oKp4iWuS00=) My name is Conor O’Callaghan. Last year, I ran against Marlene in AZ-01 and we were fierce opponents. Now, I’m endorsing her campaign for Congress. **[_Before I get into why, will you consider pitching in $5 or any amount you can to help Marlene defeat far-right Republican David Schweikert and flip the House?_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111430226/557224705/-74077192?amount=5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.07.13_Conor-OCallaghan-Endorsement_B1_F1_S1_C1_AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYwODgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMjBkYWU0ZWItNGM2MC1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=c9PxmuBgB19ybgOLrNg5jb8NWQJtGuKn4oKp4iWuS00=)** | [CHIP IN $5](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111430227/557224706/-74077192?amount=5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.07.13_Conor-OCallaghan-Endorsement_B1_F1_S1_C1_AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYwODgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMjBkYWU0ZWItNGM2MC1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=c9PxmuBgB19ybgOLrNg5jb8NWQJtGuKn4oKp4iWuS00=)
---
Here’s why Marlene has earned my endorsement:
We are at a critical point in history. I think it is high time that we ran someone from this district who has demonstrated an ability to define and battle David Schweikert.
Someone who can appeal to moderate Republicans in a district where Democrats are hemorrhaging voters. Someone who can take a hit — _or several!_ — and keep on fighting.
That is why I am backing my former rival for AZ-01. We may have once been fierce competitors, but now, I’m proud to support Marlene as she enters the arena once again.
**[_Will you join me in supporting Marlene’s campaign to unseat Schweikert and take back the House majority? Chip in $5 or anything you can today._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111430228/557224707/-74077192?amount=5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.07.13_Conor-OCallaghan-Endorsement_B1_F1_S1_C1_AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYwODgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMjBkYWU0ZWItNGM2MC1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=c9PxmuBgB19ybgOLrNg5jb8NWQJtGuKn4oKp4iWuS00=)**

clean_body: We are at a critical point in history. | | = = My name is Conor O’Callaghan. Last year, I ran against Marlene in AZ-01 and we were fierce opponents. Now, I’m endorsing her campaign for Congress. = | = Here’s why Marlene has earned my endorsement: We are at a critical point in history. I think it is high time that we ran someone from this district who has demonstrated an ability to define and battle David Schweikert. Someone who can appeal to moderate Republicans in a district where Democrats are hemorrhaging voters. Someone who can take a hit — or several! — and keep on fighting. That is why I am backing my former rival for AZ-01. We may have once been fierce competitors, but now, I’m proud to support Marlene as she enters the arena once again. = If you’ve saved your payment info with ActBlue, your contribution will be processed immediately. | | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you, Conor O’Callaghan | To learn more about Marlene, visit: We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to PAID FOR BY MARLENE FOR CONGRESS Marlene for Congress 7904 E. My mid-month deadline is less than 48 hours away, and I’m still short on a few critical grassroots donations from your state.

clean_body: I’m sorry. But sometimes, I don’t have another option. | | | | Friend, do you hate it when I ask for money? I get it — and I’m sorry. But sometimes, I just don’t have another option. Right now, I desperately need your help! My mid-month deadline is less than 48 hours away, and I’m still short on a few critical grassroots donations from your state. Will you lend me a hand? You've stood by me through EVERYTHING, and I’m truly grateful. I wish I had time to thank you in person, but I’m busy Saving America. As soon as your contribution processes, I’ll enter you to win the ONLY set of GOLD GOLF BALLS signed by me in existence. ** -** Thank you, kindly! Donald J. | | | | | | | | | | |
#### Welcome!
---
| Thank you for stepping up and joining this movement. I’m honored to welcome you aboard!
As a Marine Corps veteran, husband, father, and proud son of St. Cloud, I believe in service that puts community before politics. This campaign isn’t about climbing ladders — it’s about restoring common sense, protecting freedom, securing our borders, and fighting for our families and Veterans of Central Florida.
In the coming weeks, you’ll get updates from me – straight from the trail – where we stand, where we’re going, and how you can help take back the seat in **Florida’s 9th District**. Please take the time to give me your input and opinions. I am listening.
We’re building something real. Grassroots. Relentless. Unafraid.
With your help, we’re going to win!
Welcome to the mission!
In Service to you,

Justin Story
Husband. Dad. Marine. UF Gator. Fighter Pilot. Local.

name: Troy Downing
subject: The School of Hard Knocks
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
I’m a living testament to the old adage of pulling yourself up by the bootstraps.

Peter,
I'm a living testament to the old adage of pulling yourself up by the bootstraps
I was born to an unwed teenage mother and raised with nothing: __no wealth, no connections.__
But I had something more powerful: __grit, faith, and a country that rewards hard work.__
I seized every opportunity, fought through every setback, and built a life far beyond what anyone thought possible.[](https://trk.emails.troydowning._bsvdDLGH1g)I worked my way into college, earned a place as a research scientist at New York University, and eventually launched my own company, one I would go on to merge with Yahoo! during the tech boom of the ’90s.

I lived the American Dream, and I’m profoundly grateful for it. **But I didn’t stop there.**

I wanted to pay it forward, so I focused on angel investing and mentoring early-stage tech ventures, helping the next generation of innovators chase their own American Dream.[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.-ufqKg)I was upset after September 11th, like the rest of America. I knew had to do something. **So I joined the Air Force and served in combat search and rescue missions in Afghanistan,** putting my skills to work saving lives on the front lines of the War on Terror.| [](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)| [](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)
---|---
I’ll be honest, Peter. **_This country’s been good to me. _**

**I think about that young girl, unsure of her future, suddenly responsible for raising a child. But she gave me life. She gave me faith. This country gave me an opportunity. And God gave me a purpose.**

And now, just six months ago, that same woman flew to Washington, D.C., and watched her son get sworn into the United States Congress.
[](https://trk.emails.troydowning._a0Ivwg8UeZQ8N4GPC2VVQ)
I want to make sure the America that gave me a chance is still here for the next kid born to a single mom, with no family connections, no money, no safety net—just a dream and the determination to chase it. **But today, I worry we’re letting that promise slip away.**

We must fight to take it back. It starts with restoring fiscal discipline and reining in the chaos so the United States remains what it was always meant to be: __a shining beacon of freedom and opportunity.__

The brass ring keeps moving farther out of reach. Out-of-control spending and reckless policies are making it harder for everyday Americans to build a life.

**_Starting a family business in tech or any field shouldn’t feel impossible. Buying a first home, the foundation of the American Dream, shouldn’t be out of reach._**

We need a country where the American Dream is alive and within reach.

[Will you join me in making the American Dream a reality? Together, we can reclaim our country and ensure America remains the beacon of hope and opportunity it was always meant to be.](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)
[SUPPORT TROY DOWNING TODAY
](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)
For faith, freedom, and the American Dream,
[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)
**Troy Downing**
_Conservative in Congress_
|
---

#
* * *
---
|
---
### **What Hoosiers Need to Know About the Federal "Big Beautiful Budget Bill" and it's Impact on Indiana**
Despite the name, the so-called "Big Beautiful Budget Bill" is a disaster for working Hoosiers. It imposes the largest reduction of federal entitlement programs in U.S. history and enables the largest transfer of wealth from working families to the ultra-wealthy. Here's how it hits Indiana:
### **🚨 Health Care: Cuts That Put Lives at Risk**
Over 2 million Hoosiers rely on some version of a Medicaid plan, including kids, seniors and people with disabilities. This bill:
* Forces adults aged 19-64 in Medicaid expansion to meet new work reporting rules. Even though 92% of eligible adults already work or are exempt, these new requirements would push 5.2 million Americans off Medicaid by 2034
* Adds $35 per-visit fees for surgeries, ER visits, imaging and specialist care--penalizing patients for seeking help
* Caps federal provider tax programs like Indiana's Hospital Assessment Fee (HAF), threatening $640 million in annual Medicaid funding our hospitals depend on to stay open
* Adds costs on top of Indiana's existing $2.4 billion budget shortfall, with no clear way to fill the gap

📉 The result? Hoosiers will either lose health coverage or see their care options vanish as hospitals buckle under financial strain
### **🍎 Food Assistance: Less Help, More Hoops**
Almost 590,000 Hoosiers rely on SNAP (food stamps)--most are kids, seniors or low-wage workers. This bill:
* Cuts the federal government's share of SNAP administrative funding from 50% to 25%, leaving Indiana to pay the other 75%. That's an extra $46-50 million per year
* For the first time ever, requires states to help pay for actual SNAP benefits. With Indiana's payment error rate over 8%, the state could owe up to $216 million per year
* Shrinks eligibility, narrows exemptions and expands work requirements--meaning thousands of Hoosiers will lose food assistance even when they qualify
* Slashes funding for nutrition education and locks SNAP increases to inflation only, ignoring the actual cost of groceries

📉 The result? Fewer Hoosiers eating dinner, more red tape and up to $190 million in new state costs with no path to pay for them
### **💰 Tax Policy: A Gift to the Rich**
The bill delivers massive tax benefits to high-income Americans, while nearly 30% of Indiana families are left behind:
* Raises the estate and gift tax exemption to $15 million
* Expands the Child Tax Credit to $2,200--but only gives the full amount to families earning over $41,500
* Expands the SALT deduction cap from $10K to $40K, which helps wealthier households in high-tax states. But in Indiana, only 5% of households even claim SALT deductions, with an average benefit under $8,000

📉 The result? Working families lose care and food assistance so billionaires can pass on more wealth--and the national debt still goes up by $3.4 trillion
### **🌍 Energy and Environment: Progress Reversed**
* Wipes out unspent Inflation Reduction Act funds for clean energy, environmental protections and data systems
* Weakens methane pollution rules and removes clean energy priorities from federal energy projects
* Requires new fossil fuel and timber lease sales--including on federal lands near Hoosier communities and national forests

📉 The result? Higher pollution, slower grid modernization and less protection for Hoosier farmers, workers and families facing climate-related threats
### **Bottom Line: It Hurts the People Who Can Least Afford It**
In a state where more than 55% of households earn less than $55,000/year, this bill could not come at a worse time. It:
* Forces hard-working Hoosiers off their health insurance
* Makes food harder to afford
* Cuts support for rural hospitals and public health
* And gives nothing in return--except tax breaks for people already at the top

### **Senate Democrats Are Demanding Action**
We're calling for a public hearing within 30 days to assess the full fiscal and human impact of this bill on Indiana. That means hearing from:
* FSSA and Indiana Department of Health
* The state budget office and independent economists
* Hospital systems
* And most importantly: the Hoosiers directly impacted by these changes

# Speaking to Foster Youth at IYAB's 10th Annual Youth Conference
---
| | |
---
I was honored to once again have the opportunity to speak in front of Foster Success at the Indiana Youth Advisory Board (IYAB) conference

This conference is an event designed by IYAB members that provides resources, education and tools to young people (age 14-22) with experience in the Foster Care system.

Having spent time in the foster system and being adopted, this event is one of my favorites of the year.

I was reminded by one of the attendees this year that even though every journey is different, every journey matters. It is a message that has stuck with me since the event, and one that I will carry with me going forward.
* * *
---
| | | |
---
| | **The WNBA All-Star Game is happening right here in District 46 this week on July 19. **

I cannot wait for our city to be on the national stage, once again showing why we are a destination time and time again for major sporting events, conferences and so much more.

While our Hoosier Hospitality will be on full display as we host visitors from all over the country, there are plenty of opportunities for you and your family to enjoy the festivities taking place in our city over the next week.

>From free concerts, art exhibits, pick-up basketball games and more, there is something for everyone as we celebrate women's sports, the league's biggest stars and our great city of Indianapolis.
**[WNBA All-Star Events around Indianapolis](https://click.subscription.in.gov/?qs=8c607d73f2a50086ea597d02b998419f8060637d847a00fe9dc7d44aaa2ceb0d13425c7670c91fd27d4b82eb86bbc0506527bbf56e28d7bc "WNBA All-Star Events around Indianapolis")**

Keep an eye on my social media for more WNBA All-Star Content over the next week. I am so excited to join you in showing off the Circle City.

# The Summer Can Bring High, Dangerous Temperatures: _Make Sure You Are Prepared_
---
During extreme weather, all Indy parks family centers and branches of the Indianapolis public library are available as cooling centers.

**These can be located at: ** [Indy Parks Website](https://click.subscription.in.gov/?qs=00a817ab5a394bec970239146c39a7e1384fda4b8a8fe8f880d07a9a76572839e6861258dd799ffd8e158bf471235509068328c12e68c25f "Indy Parks Website")

[Indianapolis Public Libraries Website](https://click.subscription.in.gov/?qs=00a817ab5a394bec138fb22334ecf522b79a292df907e33573a3369ee3f2407e08a6f680c8b3522a50fbe2632237c87c80d9bec904e8b78b "Indianapolis Public Libraries Website")
### **Know the Signs of Heat Related Illness**

**_Heat Cramps_**

_Signs:_ Muscle pains or spasms in the stomach, arms, or legs.

Actions: Go to a cooler location. Remove excess clothing. Take sips of cool sports drinks with salt and sugar. Get medical help if cramps last more than an hour.

**_Heat Exhaustion_**

_Signs:_ Heavy sweating, paleness, muscle cramps, tiredness, weakness, dizziness, headache, nausea or vomiting, fainting.

Actions: Go to an air-conditioned place and lie down. Loosen or remove clothing. Take a cool bath. Take sips of cool sports drinks with salt and sugar. Get medical help if symptoms get worse or last more than an hour.

**_Heat Stroke_**

_Signs_ : Extremely high body temperature (above 103 degrees) indicated by an oral thermometer; red, hot, and dry skin with no sweat; rapid, strong pulse; dizziness; confusion; and unconsciousness.

_Actions:_ Call 9-1-1 or get the person to a hospital immediately. Cool down with whatever methods are available until medical help arrives.

This past session my colleagues and I worked hard to support legislation that will better Indiana for all who call it home.

Whether it is **addressing the health care needs of both the present and future** , **protecting consumers in the landscape of emerging industries** or**working to improve the lives of the most vulnerable** , the Senate Democrats spent this past session fighting for every Hoosier family.

In the same breath, we fought hard all session against legislation that takes us backwards as a state and neglects those who need the most help.

The Indiana Senate Democrats are already hard at work connecting with constituents to inform our legislative priorities for next session as we continue fighting for the future Hoosiers deserve.

It is a message that has stuck with me since the event, and one that I will carry with me going forward. | | | | | | The WNBA All-Star Game is happening right here in District 46 this week on July 19. I cannot wait for our city to be on the national stage, once again showing why we are a destination time and time again for major sporting events, conferences and so much more. While our Hoosier Hospitality will be on full display as we host visitors from all over the country, there are plenty of opportunities for you and your family to enjoy the festivities taking place in our city over the next week. From free concerts, art exhibits, pick-up basketball games and more, there is something for everyone as we celebrate women's sports, the league's biggest stars and our great city of Indianapolis. "WNBA All-Star Events around Indianapolis" Additionally, IndyGo is offering free rides during All-Star Weekend, find out more "here". Keep an eye on my social media for more WNBA All-Star Content over the next week. I am so excited to join you in showing off the Circle City. | | | | | | Calling all young professionals! This is a great opportunity to make your voice heard regarding the future of our city. This event is hosted by the Indy Chamber and is a wonderful networking opportunity and a chance for your to have an impact on what Indy will look like for years to come. "Register Here" The Summer Can Bring High, Dangerous Temperatures: Make Sure You Are Prepared During extreme weather, all Indy parks family centers and branches of the Indianapolis public library are available as cooling centers. These can be located at: "Indy Parks Website" "Indianapolis Public Libraries Website" Know the Signs of Heat Related Illness Heat Cramps Signs: Muscle pains or spasms in the stomach, arms, or legs. Actions: Go to a cooler location. Remove excess clothing. Take sips of cool sports drinks with salt and sugar. Get medical help if cramps last more than an hour. Heat Exhaustion Signs: Heavy sweating, paleness, muscle cramps, tiredness, weakness, dizziness, headache, nausea or vomiting, fainting. Actions: Go to an air-conditioned place and lie down. Loosen or remove clothing. Take a cool bath. Take sips of cool sports drinks with salt and sugar. Get medical help if symptoms get worse or last more than an hour. Heat Stroke Signs : Extremely high body temperature (above 103 degrees) indicated by an oral thermometer; red, hot, and dry skin with no sweat; rapid, strong pulse; dizziness; confusion; and unconsciousness. Actions: Call 9-1-1 or get the person to a hospital immediately. Cool down with whatever methods are available until medical help arrives. Above you can find a quick breakdown of some our wins from this legislative session, as well as some of the legislation that we advocated against. This past session my colleagues and I worked hard to support legislation that will better Indiana for all who call it home. Whether it is addressing the health care needs of both the present and future , protecting consumers in the landscape of emerging industries orworking to improve the lives of the most vulnerable , the Senate Democrats spent this past session fighting for every Hoosier family. In the same breath, we fought hard all session against legislation that takes us backwards as a state and neglects those who need the most help. The Indiana Senate Democrats are already hard at work connecting with constituents to inform our legislative priorities for next session as we continue fighting for the future Hoosiers deserve. For more information on these bills, or any others, visit "iga.in.gov". | | | | | News Around Our State | | | "" "$" "$" "$" COMMUNITY RESOURCES To view my community resources guide, "click here. " | | | | | | | | | Created and distributed by the Indiana Senate Democratic Communications office mailto:Nancy.Singh@iga.in.gov mailto:Chester.Bryant@iga.in.gov mailto:Molly.Neary@iga.in.gov ---|---|--- | This email was sent by: Indiana General Assembly 200 W. Washington St. Indianapolis, IN, 46204, US

# [What is Trump hiding? Tell him to release the Epstein files](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0ae440a2-0617-41ba-b37d-a17314125e8e/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

---
| | | After years of outlandish claims about the Epstein files implicating Democrats, Donald Trump is suddenly doing whatever he can to keep them from being released -- including ([reportedly](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb2b75ff0-7689-4f79-94bd-38351736ddf9/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)) ordering Attorney General Pam Bondi to keep them secret. (And he's even [more unhinged than usual](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tcbfbbcab-11dc-477e-a724-16f3d8ac897d/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) when it comes to this subject on social media). Could it be he is really, really, really scared? And that his ex-BroFriend Elon Musk lit that fire of fear when he told the world that Trump himself was in those files? Enough speculating, and enough obfuscating -- Trump needs to release ALL those files NOW. America has the right to know if its president is a pedophile. Even some of the MAGA's are breaking with Trump on this, demanding the files be made public. [_**What is Trump hiding? Add your name and tell him to release the Epstein files!**_](https://default.salsalabs.org/T9fd75eba-c475-4b30-9386-b7daaec4c0c8/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

# Monday, July 14, 2025

## Wildfires Tear Through Grand Canyon’s North Rim
The historic [Grand Canyon Lodge on the North Rim has been destroyed](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFvgzAQBeBfAxvofBg7HTx0YUvXjuiCz-EqgyPbCuLfV0l_QucnvafvfV_nK0mU_f5FG7sa0rzzUSLXynm-Z9r9QvuZ9kOiD5K5PFKpNCPg2IHtlJ49STxn3XoXmJGHlp2yI-qL1ZexXR0bY4EsAPMAdPNGm49Fh2E0pBQa3Yp7tYFVWgFoPfZ0Q9RqIBj9wKMNjQb2UlPuf5LsdeUQz_Tk3C9pa6Nba32UZvhscGpwOo6jP2lN6ZU2OL04DU4pBFmEYlfoLN0qpaYsS_cWdn_ETlm8qAEA-7Vusc3OPw6JUYqxjYb7RhLfk_VfPz0d_gYAAP__0IN8RA) by wildfire, along with the visitor center, gas station, wastewater plant, and dozens of other structures, park officials said Sunday. The Dragon Bravo Fire was [originally managed as a controlled burn](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO4yAURb8Gd1iPZwhOQbGNu2y7pUXMw2aHQAQklv9-5MwnTH2lo3vOv9t8syGGtP61DzLN5znRXiO1RmVei01usenIaQ_R-VCoPnNtdkZAxUFzIWdnQzxm2TnjiZCGjozQCuWo5ai6zQh1JbWMQNqLC8lBXDSgoHG5C7h677pgThpoIQWAlKq3d0QpBgvKDaS0ZxLIhZZL_z-H1Dby8chvKv2SH100W2vPyoY_DCeG077v_VeO7hwZTiea4QSa4SQGhpMrds2J34t9Z34acUe1lXxUroCPwGsrr6W9ClX-8ec_ASpPubSNl3Biu2Lccw8xhnrRTML6sCF-_rRfRXwb_A4AAP__eACFXg) to clear brush and reduce future fire risk, but it escaped control last week when high temperatures, gusty winds, and dry conditions fueled its rapid spread to more than 5,000 acres. Meanwhile, the [nearby White Sage Fire](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFu8yAQwPGnwRsWnI-QDAzf4i3f2tHC5kioCFhA4_rtK6ftE3T7Syfd6Xdv1-lqQwzp9t8-yDSfp0RbjdQalelWbHKLTXtOW4jOh0J1zbXZCQQoLjSXODkb4j5h54wnAho6MlIrwLPGs-ruRuFFL_PJa5jhBFpq8AqEp0G6GS5-6II5tgktUQqBqHo7A6AcrFBuIKU9Q0EutFz69xxSu5OPe35S6Zf86KK5t7ZWNvxjMDIYt23r07wcimPOYDySwfhR-U_9WvjLx7-BnD5Xm1zlZUkW5BlBdsW4dQsxhnrSDMXtYUN8HW1_-tTTwFcAAAD___64fXc)—sparked by lightning during a recent thunderstorm—has grown to over 19,000 acres near Jacob Lake, forcing evacuations and stretching fire crews thin. As of Sunday afternoon, both fires remained uncontained, and the entire North Rim of Grand Canyon National Park is closed until further notice.
## New Flood Threat Pauses Texas Search Efforts
Officials in Central Texas [halted search efforts](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDFuxCAURE-DOyzAsKwLijTuNm1KC8PH_hEGC8g6e_vIe4W0b6TRzPt6zA-LEdP6aXcwLeQ5wVkjtAZlXotN3tn0yunE6AMWqEeuzc6CCUWZplzO3mJ8zbLzJgAIGDowXCsh71reVbcZyZlWSxjCCDd_49wtelQjZ86HkY1y7NBcbUxzyRmTUvV2EULywTLlB1A6EMnAY8ul_86Y2gYhvvITSu_y3kWztXZUMnwQMRExnefZp8VdL678ImDbBoWIKcScfSViavBrKw3R1o2-IaaVVrDFbfSJruFeaf2pByQPnhaXrOB3xWVXjD9OjBHrTRPJ1t1ifM9o_3L3NOIvAAD__4OhhLA) for victims of the July 4 floods, as another flood threat hit the affected area Sunday. Some areas reported 6 to 10 inches of rain, and the Lampasas and San Saba rivers [surged](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykULFuxCAU-5pkIyIEwmVg6JLtunaM3sEjoSJwgqdL8_dVep_QyZZsWba_7ssdQgxp_YQdDfm8JDxqRCIsy1ogOQvpzOkI0flQsD5zJVgEF4pxzXq5OAjxXGTrjEcUOLRoeq2EvGl5U-1mwHtr5TAOehpxmtSolNXoJn0TFvjo2mCuNK572XMupergIYTsB-DKDai0byRHFyiX7juHRBv6eOYXls7mvY1mI3rWZvhoxNyI-TiOzj7qteLSGzFf9A2M8Acq8zFnx2grCMQ2iFRZRSh2Y69gKeyVOQQXz7cxpLURc1uMex4hxlBH3Ui-7hDiXwH612svI34DAAD__xt1g4Y) about 30 inches, causing authorities to direct all search crews to “evacuate the river corridor” at around 8 a.m. local time. Heavy rains lingered over Texas Hill Country through Sunday morning, with a flash flood warning in effect until about noon local time. Over 160 people are still missing, including about a dozen from Camp Mystic.
## Two Killed in Kentucky Church Shooting
[Two women were killed in a shooting](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkb2O2zAQhJ-G6mTwZ_VzhYoYhru7NqWwIpfSxhQpkNQZfvvAKQKkTjn4gAG-mZ-f8ydy4Lh-4U5T9WmO9CyBaqU8rxmjsxhfKT45OM-ZypFKxVlL3bVyaBXMDjm8Zmjc5Ik0mYYmNXQaxgHGrtkmtyjtvaHFfABYWPQIyilpTCc9jpoant5tclCgpAToLrhoDcqg7JyhbvACJDmuKV9-JY51Ix9e6Zvyxaa9CdNW61GE-SH0Xeg7Hm-BN3qHXNkGEvr-oFhP-3i1djuz3dqypVQ5rm05y0G2to7QtdYoUlZZ2ckFP3pQvV-clqMmGPvegTD3s-5zSWe2JMwtRSq8RgxC97gfwlzfeCfH5y7M7TjL9g-xuB_IaxTm9ndB014z4YPjKvT1i56lyZM7nhwCl34QINcdOfxxrf910PekfwcAAP__L_akDg) at Richmond Road Baptist Church in Lexington, Kentucky, after a suspect shot and wounded a state trooper near the airport. The suspect then carjacked a vehicle and fled to the church, where the shooting occurred. Authorities believe the suspect may have had a connection to the victims at the church, but the motive remains under investigation. The [suspect was later killed by police](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOwyAQAF9jd0SAsUkKimvS5dorLeJd25tgQLBO5N-fcl-4eqTRaH5u481ToLh8-w0dz2mM-K4BmbGMS_ERJh-PFN8UYKaCNafKftRS90JaocwInsIxmhbcjKixa9Ep22tztubct6s7Q3fxKL21eu7kxcCgwFh5Gex9lmBkS-5jk1YZJaUx_cnftTaq87KHDns7N0YiEKdyeiSKvOIcjvTCcprS1ga3MufadF-Nvjb6mjHlgB_U6GtdU2KKi7iHHcVSfK3CU8mpsHhi5H16HmJa9zKtYtsDUw4oKD72giDqXjNOLAA9CKWsVWoY2uIgvykEqoNtjFw2T-EvhP917-X0bwAAAP__1N2FuQ) in a standoff. Governor Andy Beshear praised the swift response of law enforcement, while the investigation continues.

clean_body: The historic by wildfire, along with the visitor center, gas station, wastewater plant, and dozens of other structures, park officials said Sunday. | | | | | | Monday, July 14, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1789, revolutionaries stormed the Bastille prison in Paris, igniting the French Revolution and toppling centuries of absolute monarchy. Watch a . Today we’re reporting on a with panoramic Grand Teton views and a pet spa, because of course it has a pet spa. If The Flyover were handing out blank checks (we’re not, we’re sorry, we wish we could), where would you buy your ideal vacation home? Reply to this email and let us know. Today's sponsor is world-renowned doctor and nutritionist . | Wildfires Tear Through Grand Canyon’s North Rim The historic by wildfire, along with the visitor center, gas station, wastewater plant, and dozens of other structures, park officials said Sunday. The Dragon Bravo Fire was to clear brush and reduce future fire risk, but it escaped control last week when high temperatures, gusty winds, and dry conditions fueled its rapid spread to more than 5,000 acres. Meanwhile, the —sparked by lightning during a recent thunderstorm—has grown to over 19,000 acres near Jacob Lake, forcing evacuations and stretching fire crews thin. As of Sunday afternoon, both fires remained uncontained, and the entire North Rim of Grand Canyon National Park is closed until further notice. New Flood Threat Pauses Texas Search Efforts Officials in Central Texas for victims of the July 4 floods, as another flood threat hit the affected area Sunday. Some areas reported 6 to 10 inches of rain, and the Lampasas and San Saba rivers about 30 inches, causing authorities to direct all search crews to “evacuate the river corridor” at around 8 a.m. local time. Heavy rains lingered over Texas Hill Country through Sunday morning, with a flash flood warning in effect until about noon local time. Over 160 people are still missing, including about a dozen from Camp Mystic. Two Killed in Kentucky Church Shooting at Richmond Road Baptist Church in Lexington, Kentucky, after a suspect shot and wounded a state trooper near the airport. The suspect then carjacked a vehicle and fled to the church, where the shooting occurred. Authorities believe the suspect may have had a connection to the victims at the church, but the motive remains under investigation. The in a standoff. Governor Andy Beshear praised the swift response of law enforcement, while the investigation continues. () | Flying together with our sponsor If it feels like your body fights you every time you try to lose weight... You're not imagining it. According to one of Beverly Hills' leading cosmetic surgeons, it may be due to a biological phenomenon known as the "Fat-Lock Effect." Studies show that under certain conditions, your body can store extra calories as fat without eating a single extra bite. That means if you eat three meals a day, your body is reacting as if you ate a fourth meal and hoarding the excess calories. But here's the good news: Dr. John Lakey has recently discovered a simple 2-minute daily ritual that helps counteract the "fat-lock effect." **Good Morning!** On this day in 1879, the Texas legislature authorized selling state land in 52 western counties for [fifty cents an acre](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGSrCAQgOHTYKYFDY5jQPASs3nphlYjjVKLMIW943j7rd0r7AX-v-r7eMwPjCnm9T_uZPkdypzpPBIxU51zWUoO0VNe6FWYdlzRfdWVZpDQt3JolZk9xnTNpvHWST-qhqwaejCjHNWt2ezdgSbswXnq71oiACHS4MdFKTfedBPtT0wOyiglVW86NKMDHXTQELyXKIwkH7nUjjdieuMR0lVeVLul7E2yG_PzEPqfgEnAdJ5nx8eGJaeYqSt1FTBtmL0r5VPARJlrpEPAFGLgq10oc4sLN9X65xlTisdtEEauO8b0e-C_ubwsfAcAAP__0v944w). The law was repealed four years later due to rampant fraud. Read why a Canadian couple [travels 1,400 miles](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGOwyAUBNDT4A4LMDFxQbGNu2y7pfUN3zZaDBH82MntV8kVth7pzWh-btMNQgxp_YYdLT2XPCU8a0QiLFPKLqcleEwOj0y4wwrzo6w4KaEuXBgu9eQhxNekG29n4QfZoJXmovQgBtk3mx1Ej33feeMM-KvvUc94BcB57mYjnW6CfWPCSC2lkBfdgh5m1S3d0qnFewFMC_SBcmlpQ8In1CW-8oGldXlvot2I7pV1X0yNTI3neba_kY53yNT4tpkahWFqlIKpcUckHkPyELFyBwl8gMRdftwj8nPL_Ag1EEeoxD9tnPKZeN2ghLRy2jAUnmBHjuA2_kIoTI1Nsf5-hhhD7Q3TYt0hxM9A-t-th1V_AQAA__9NqI39) to Lindale every year in today's _Et Cetera_ section. We're sponsored today by [Worksport](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkEsO2yAQQE-Dd7b4GeIFi0pRdum2S4TNYKPysQA7ye0rR1Uv0O28p5nR-_XUT-ODT-tPE0G1t8s6wasGaA2KTnnJyXkLaYEzN4hmNfNRVtAU07HHsidcW-PDR_POqhnbiXSgiBwpn_BERLcpM1s-CyvBLZOYmWOCMEYAOynm2yho59W1DEvCCcFk5IPh00yZY45RZy02iGOwvuUytA0avE114ZNPKMOSYxfU1tpeEfuB6APRh08n1Da8cvld91zaJV2EPY4Wdc1HWQCxO0TjA6LiGkaw_oiI3Xfjbb-b0hKUuvn9L19M3I1f09f4B8kN9xxL_a1gNJlGIikeJ0y6ouz-8iH4KiTieL1ufZ9t_5f4VPRPAAAA___31ZC-), the revolutionary vehicle mobile power system that you can invest in.

## Rains Interrupt Flood Recovery Efforts
Heavy rains and flash flood warnings temporarily [interrupted recovery efforts](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGypCAQRb8GMy1oQMaAYBOyt-mGFkrjUIUypT06_v0W8wsv6qTP6b7338_441NO2_LXr2jpE8u44XVkJMJ93MpctpgCbjOehXD1i5_e-4IjcNAtN61QY_Ap36Nqgp14GESDVhgNauCD6JunlabXYtB-Rj7Ns0HEQQkhH702-IDYN8lWGTdCCcGFVp1XwwQyyighhsA9UxxDorJ39ETCjz9ivsuJezeXtcn2SfQ6mPzDwDFw13V178NTCf6uCwzcQWW_GbiarA5PqWwMXL3LwHHDwAnJwH3lbcylhLQtbeXWo83pxPb9Cp6w4g_NJR-05l-u2W14XSnndPSGKb6sPuXvY_S7Ok8L_wMAAP__AdOHeg) in Kerr County and Central Texas yesterday. The number of reported deaths from the disaster has reached 129, with scores still missing. Yesterday, DHS Secretary Kristi Noem dismissed as “absolutely false” claims that a new policy requiring her personal approval of large contracts at FEMA [hampered its response](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDluwzAURE9DdRS4aStYpFHntCmFL_FTJsDFICnLun1gF7lA2hlg5uH93JYbOO_i_g0BdX3ZtEQ8i8daMS8xbSlaZzBu-EwVA-ywHnnHRTDRUTZQrhYDzl-LaoxemZl4g5oPnVATm3jf3DWwSTDV8xFWGNdJ9mIcQVphpF0n3neN0-8xNnDFOeOdakFNq5BWWimsMQyIYmhcTbmtd6z4gmL9lZ6Y2y2Fxut7rY9C5BcRMxHzeZ5txqNiLu-eiLkcpYKLsDrv6kXEvHkXoCLFiHl_BzFhoAYtRlOoxQA0Y3mkWJB-_qj1KZlCIThDy5aP6uJF_xxIIuYma_M4nfeu9ANRbA_g_Aew_k_rU4vfAAAA__-TXY4z) to the floods, insisting the agency had deployed resources "an hour or two after the flooding.” FEMA reportedly [granted several appeals](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2u5CAQBODT4KxHDcbjR-BgE2dv0w0tbBoPEtAWMD_e06-8V3hxSZ-q6s_38m1DDHn_bRNN7eN5yfSukVqjsmTeOPvgKG_04kbJ7nZ9lp0WhWoAHEHqxdkQz0V3blrRGdnRJMdBaYNG3rvHZPUdDZnxrr2j_ssN6mvbsNfGDBJNj12YLgxHqaVEOeib1WZVve99r7xzaIVGcqFxubUHNfrY6uPJLyq3jVMXp0drRxX9L6FmoeZ8HlzbFQk1X7JQM45CzVIJNT8rXPuEmj0lC4USv8iB47-UK7CHzaYD0llb2GB9huhC3iv4wgkkIpxkC_jI7CDZA1byXAjoc9hcA2cotHFxl9-VyR3vEGOo91Fo3JMN8X_j9rOfX5P6FwAA___N75I_) from Camp Mystic to exclude dozens of its buildings from the agency’s 100-year flood map since 2011. In the last decade, Kerr County officials opted against using $10.2 million in federal funds to [install flood warning sirens](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFyhCAURb9GOx14giwFRRq7TZvSecJDmSDsCNHdv89sfiH1nblnzvm6z3cMMaT1E3cy9enznOgqkWqlY07Z5uSDo2TpzJV2XHH5OVaagYHsmOq4mB2G-JpF68zCnOYtGa4kCM00H9vNCBAWR6a499biIsXAb_KmFehhYIt2bTDvM6a44JxxKXoUeoHBD34A7xzDRjByoeajrxtVemLx8ZVPOnqb9zaardZHaYaPBqYGpuu6-hduOb_XBqa3TgOTC67zoViMnc2p0HFiDWXvlpjtd_eImEoHIEFwJcd-q3tsD-MeV4gxlFE1gq07hviHrP8LdRr4DQAA___UeX0V), and local authorities were unsuccessful in [securing a loan](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkT2OpDAQRk8DWSH_8DMOCFZakc2mG6LCVaatNTay3c307VfMFSZ7QemVnr6_n-sn-uDj_gcPnuuXS2vkqwSulfMak03ReeJo-ZUqH7jj9sw7r0qoAcQEsl8JfXivfUvzJsjIlmc5Dao3wsixfcyD-OjViE6hVsrayRG6yRCzMkQbja2fb5mYZC-lkEPfYW82pZ12WjkigU0vmHxNuasPrvyFxYV3enHubDraMD9qPUujfzVqadRyXVe3Y3jdGfdBo5YbVzxvwupTbNSCQGyRGJKDw5fCBOk8U67P6KvnAt9_wKZnoFjB-UggD3ApgwspEVyYo487lHepfEBkzGDxOMstz9XbwCvqzaIeNBg1GRhG8QG4IQIaFmiNQ3Kye9QjNHrZQrL_Vk-N_j1oaaRp80zn5UPwZZyaXuwH-vBdXH-202tW_wMAAP__BIWqJw) for them. [More than $30 million](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OgzAQhJ8GOiPbOPwULq6hy7VXIoPXYMnsRmYJ4e1PyT3C9d-MZr6f-3h3MUVcvt0Gll-BRoRzT8AMeUSaCUP0gDM8iWFzi5uOvMCopb4J2QplRu9iukZTejtJ36sSrGpv2vSyV0252sl4M09tY0LXmQCh7WrVdJ3sTB961zRltO8y2SqjlFQ3UznTT7oOdah18F66wkjwkSlXvALDy-0hXfSEXM20lcmuzI-9qL8KPRR6OM-zCvRq3bFzxDdS6OH9qdDDJyxCIvIRFzHTth0Y-RKBDvSOI6H4Y9aYkpjpQM6XWLJDFg6RDpxhA-QyW_84Y0pxb9rCyGVzMX3m8P8kPq3-DQAA___1TYq3) has been raised for flood victims. On Saturday, Kerrville Mayor Joe Herring Jr. announced that those [impacted by the disaster](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpDAQRL_GZB7ZjRmOgOASsr30QtTY7aG1xl6ZHlj-_sT9wsYl1at6fz_mD-TE-fUHNxrlO5Y507knEqE65-JLjhwoezqK0IYvXN71RTMY6LTptXVzQE7X7JowLiYMtqHR9h24wQz22axj79D4zg-D74cWrAs0gF_8L-zi0ronNTzeZaa3zlpjO_dANyzQxja2EEMwqJyhwFLqQ1YS-sY9pqscVB--bE0aV5GvXbW_FUwKpvM8H587yh0qmO43CqZUPCYF041SMJlewWRbBZNnuXSJ-pNqPTgl0htepWrMubyzp11nOvVJy85CWopGDnrllLQv7yz10jGVEvTBXni7UU0dw9fJKfH-7JUzrw05_Z8qPxN8jPAvAAD__y08kPk) could find support at the new website [KerrTogether.com](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykj7FuwyAQQL_GbImOA4IYGLpkS9eOCJvDRsUmwlcn-fuq7Sd0f-9J7-MWbrHUss3vcSXPz9zCRo-9EjP1sLWpbbkk2iY6GtMa5zh-9ZkCApoT2JPUIcVSX0GL5EdITgry0hrUDpy8iMUrk0bQDq3NEpwxqGgiSNo4wpiTEsX_xMBKLSVIo89RuxFVVllhTgnioIFS4dbPvBDTM-65vtpB_Ty1VVS_MN_3Qb0NeB3w-km9c5uJlz9gwKvoPt0fpdayX-ygYV5jqb8y_2_58PgdAAD__74qas0).
## Austin Firefighters Accuse Chief of Denying Aid
The Austin Firefighters Association approved a [vote of no confidence](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L8OmzAQBvCngc3I_whh8NCFLV07ogOf4SRjR_YlJG9f0Q59gK6n06fv-_16zA-gSGn7CQc6_oQ8JzxrRGYsc8prToE8phXfmfGADZZX2XDWUvdCDkLZ2QPF72xb7xbpR9WiU0Ov7ShHdWt35-_-bvQyLn0Y_WACGtTaaGV0DysuS0vuCpODskpJ1dsO7LhoE0wwOngvobESPXEuHe_I-IEa4je_sXRrPtroduZnbcyPRk-Nns7z7EL-DPCqTOl6afR0bWr09PcmAhUMtO2MpQqoNa8ETDmJghGhYhUF6ytyFSmLfwLiIhCwAaXKAoIX604Y2uL886QYqd6GxsrtAIp_qvH_gb6d_h0AAP__JPuPXw) against Austin Fire Department Chief Joel Baker last week over allegations that he denied critical assistance to Kerr County prior to the deadly flooding on July 4. The resolution, backed by 93% of members, accuses Baker of refusing requests for aid on July 2 and 3, citing a standing order to deny deployments to save money. Baker has said he didn’t receive the requests until July 4, when he approved a separate request for three rescue swimmers. Baker insists the department “absolutely” did [everything it could](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGupSAYhJ8GO04A8XgtKLaxu9tuaX7hR8lBfgN4PL79xi32BW43yUwmM9-f7-kbQgxp-Q0bmvrxNCU8S8RaMU-JLCUfHCaLb6q4wQLzkReclFAdFz2XenIQ4jXpxplZuEE2aGTfKT2IQT6b1fRffedRDBZn-RSuFWKQcpZfelZWtcI3wdxlopdaSiE7_QA9zKr1rW-Vd04A0wJdqJQfdcWKHyg-XvTG_LC0NdGste6Ftb-YGpkaz_N8vD6QbpOp8X7D1BjJQmRqhKPUkP4L7kNGbteAnjv0mFzhGctOqSAHXzFzsPYoUAOlwulORbhCWviKceeeMn9hztzSkerFfSRyIS1MjU02bj9DjKE8e6bFskGI_xbXn3F-G_U3AAD__4EylcA) to help with search and rescue operations in Kerr County while ensuring it had "sufficient resources in our own community," noting 14 first responders helped with local search efforts in the days following the flash flooding. Austin Mayor Kirk Watson and city manager T.C. Broadnax [condemned the vote](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L-SnSAUx_GnwY47cETQgiKN3aZN6fDnoGQRdhB179tnTJMHSP2dOXM-v18fy4eJKeb1p9lRt-9Qloz3kbA1rEsuruQQPWaHV2m4m9XYs664AIOBMkW5WLyJ6b2IzmvL_MQ71FwNICY2cdltWqFkE3JmAVVQbDBj8KgGq4SQVkDfRf0cY4oLzhkfxMuIyUIf-tBD8J4ZIhj62Ep9tQ0bfpsjpHe5sL5c2bukt9a-DtL_IDATmO_7fn1eJz6RwGxqiy4hgflxEZhTcSY94TxazDTEiiGuW8N60DPHkmku9B-bPm7qtoiB_i6YqDWfWAnMICfaW2nBcUcnFYCKsRd0HJyno2PDGKQ0bjRd1f7rjinFQyoi2LqbmP5-3v5v70vDnwAAAP__Y8ST-A) and issued statements of support for Baker. Watson chastised AFA President Bob Nicks for "politicizing this horrible loss," but Nicks argued the the vote of no confidence "isn’t about politics, it’s about accountability."
## Groups Step Up to Help Displaced Pets
The devastating floods in Kerr County and Central Texas displaced hundreds of pets. Many organizations are stepping up to help them find new homes or reunite them with their owners. [Best Friends Animal Society](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD1u6zAQhE9DdRSWP7KeChYPCNQ5TRCkFFZaUl6AEg2Ssa2cPrCvkHqAmfm-r_N0Ro68r--4eVcfIU27v5foa_V52tOS9sDk98XfUvUbrjh_59VPGnQnoZfKToQcj8k25GagQTXeqb7TdoBBnZqLA2tnZRQhAdLQW2MQ6J_1oLELHeiG3bMMemWVAtXZFu0waxNMMDoQAQoLnrim3NaLr_6BJcQj3Xxul7Q10V1qvRZh_gs9Cj3ivDwJ2jU9Y6HHzw-hx5RX3PkHK6ddIufIoRapDMgFa5GU1iJxY5LkkeIhXzNCj6WmfAgzMgnzprQ5ddB3qsmOrneOkcupFxbWDTm-3tS_Obw5_RsAAP__9o6FQw) and [Wings of Rescue](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2u6yAQBeDV2J0tfsYhFBSvSZfX3hIRmHGQMERA4mT3V84WbjfSHH3SOT9Xe3Uxxbz-dxua_qZiM-4tYe9YbS6-ZIoBs8dX6bi51d2edUUrmFgmpiYONriYPhbGYG4saD6i4WoRoJnmp_Fu3FmTJqWQggJ51h68V0uQSAwZgBqjOTCmOHDO-AKzA30TkiRJQSEwNwDDEHupc79jx7drlD7lhXX2ZRuTuff-aIP8N4jLIC77vs9U3nDUOAKDuBzn8Yl5bVOhqWLzT5y-1ESplNDGasJjjynFdlIDsHVzMX39_rdVXkb8BgAA___gMHhG) have airlifted around 250 pets from local shelters to make room for those displaced by the flooding. Austin Pets Alive! and Kerrville Pets Alive! have together [rescued around 400 pets](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOhCAURb9GOyaAIEtBsc10s-2WBuGhL0EwgDrz95uZX9j6JvecnN_H9LAYMS0_dgPTniFPCa4aoTUoU8oup4AekoMzN9jsYuejLDBxyiWhijAxeYvxNYnem5l6zXowTEkuNNVs7FfjBfMgvmZHueZcSwF6mCWnDmYFo3I9mvcZVUwwRpkUNyv0zIcwhIEH76ntBAWPLZdbW6HB09YQX_mEcnN566NZW9trN3x3_N7x-w55j_CeOn63CTcbSYHqDiABk6-kwIGpQSWSbFgrpoU42yq51kzqUU48wZMPhoSYs6-EMaWolGNfjN8vjBHrqDpBl81i_Ei0_6U7Df8LAAD__8wdhMU) from the affected areas. Some pets were able to fend for themselves. Austin Firefighters Accuse Chief of Denying Aid The Austin Firefighters Association approved a against Austin Fire Department Chief Joel Baker last week over allegations that he denied critical assistance to Kerr County prior to the deadly flooding on July 4. The resolution, backed by 93% of members, accuses Baker of refusing requests for aid on July 2 and 3, citing a standing order to deny deployments to save money. Baker has said he didn’t receive the requests until July 4, when he approved a separate request for three rescue swimmers. Baker insists the department “absolutely” did to help with search and rescue operations in Kerr County while ensuring it had "sufficient resources in our own community," noting 14 first responders helped with local search efforts in the days following the flash flooding. Austin Mayor Kirk Watson and city manager T.C. Broadnax and issued statements of support for Baker. Watson chastised AFA President Bob Nicks for "politicizing this horrible loss," but Nicks argued the the vote of no confidence "isn’t about politics, it’s about accountability." Groups Step Up to Help Displaced Pets The devastating floods in Kerr County and Central Texas displaced hundreds of pets. Many organizations are stepping up to help them find new homes or reunite them with their owners. and have airlifted around 250 pets from local shelters to make room for those displaced by the flooding. name: Team Johnny O
subject: ICYMI: Johnny O Joins Effort to Ban Stock Trading by Members of Congress
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Johnny O for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3]

As a public official, Johnny works for the best interests of his constituents and country – not his bank account.
But amid reports of other lawmakers profiting from insider knowledge – like the ups and downs of President Trump’s tariff policies – it’s no wonder trust in Congress is at an all time low. One member of Congress is under fire for dumping his stock in a Medicaid health provider just before he voted for historic Medicaid cuts.
Study after study show members of Congress outperform the market average.
And Johnny’s not having it.
The Baltimore Sun just covered his efforts to end insider trading within Congress. He’s cosponsoring legislation that would require members, their spouses and children to divest their stocks or move them to a blind trust.
Will you donate today to support Johnny in this fight for accountability? Just $10 can help him restore public confidence in our public servants. [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3]

* * *
# [A Nation of Need](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=168125946&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MTI1OTQ2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI0OTQ3OTcsImV4cCI6MTc1NTA4Njc5NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.pOv5s-mIYXN_phEbfuqM1H4175ljyzmZubN1ENUWuY0)
| | [Andrew Yang](https://substack.com/@andrewyang)
---
| Jul 14
---
| [](https://substack.com/@andrewyang)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=168125946&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MTI1OTQ2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1MjQ5NDc5NywiZXhwIjoxNzU1MDg2Nzk3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjg3MzA0Iiwic3ViIjoicmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.EPbaYjm7wybcajJiu0YIrh5G0VNEsPqUo4_Q9bIsjIE&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=168125946&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MTI1OTQ2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI0OTQ3OTcsImV4cCI6MTc1NTA4Njc5NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.pOv5s-mIYXN_phEbfuqM1H4175ljyzmZubN1ENUWuY0&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=168125946&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MTI1OTQ2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI0OTQ3OTcsImV4cCI6MTc1NTA4Njc5NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.pOv5s-mIYXN_phEbfuqM1H4175ljyzmZubN1ENUWuY0)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvYW5kcmV3eWFuZy9wL2EtbmF0aW9uLW9mLW5lZWQ_.2ZrBxdDsSttVuomnP-unagB7xeelU-Hv8yUcmWh4jDI?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/andrewyang/p/a-nation-of-need?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

A [study](https://substack.com/redirect/51575ffe-13f4-42b1-a5cf-30293a6a6f34?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) recently came out that said that 60% of Americans can't afford a decent standard of living. That is, 60% of Americans can't afford things like basic leisure activities.
I read this and felt saddened by it, if not fully surprised. Things are tough for most families out there, and getting worse.
The [Big Dark Bill](https://substack.com/redirect/dd50481c-cd0f-4284-86e1-d32838601c5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) will compound the problem. Food banks are expecting a surge in the hungry even as their own resources from government will be reduced. 42 million Americans are on SNAP, which you likely know of as Food Stamps.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/16690904-e858-4f33-bc14-156871afbbaf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
One way that government benefits will change is that there will be more paperwork to fill out for many Americans. "We're building AI tools to help people navigate the forms and requirements to access benefits," said Jimmy Chen, the founder and CEO of [Propel](https://substack.com/redirect/907357a6-b049-4589-afa2-192e18fcee5c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), whom I interviewed on the podcast this week.
I have admired Propel for a long time. "We now have 5 million households using Propel, which given that many of those households include children we touch more than 10 million Americans a month," Jimmy relates.
Jimmy grew up the child of immigrants in Kansas City. His family had some hardship growing up, but Jimmy excelled in school and got a full ride to Stanford. He started working at Facebook in the Bay Area, but that didn't seem like the right fit. "I wanted to solve problems that affected a lot of ordinary people, like my family growing up." So he moved to Brooklyn to join a fellowship program to solve problems that faced the poor.
"I interviewed a ton of people who were on SNAP and found out the only way for them to check their balance was to call a 1-800 number and put in their information. It was the kind of friction that makes everything more difficult, because you have to go through this process every time you went to the grocery store. So we found a way to pull the data and display it on an app. The app is free for users. We went to benefits offices and told people about it. As soon as we got a few thousand users they told other people, because it made their life easier and better. Pretty soon, we had hundreds of thousands of users. We then found other ways to help them, like reducing theft and making it easier for them to apply for jobs. Thousands of people have found jobs through Propel."
Jimmy is humble about his journey, but he's spent more than a decade building Propel and has learned a lot. "In my opinion, people talk about the laws and the policy, but then they ignore the implementation and experience. Poor people deal with a lot. Imagine if everything that the government did worked as well as a banking app, and the forms were easy or filled out for you. Americans would have a lot more confidence in government's ability to solve problems." He's right about that.
It reminds me of the goals of the [Abundance](https://substack.com/redirect/dca0c1de-517d-48bd-a9c8-afc8df103120?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) movement, which is largely about reducing red tape so the government can deliver on its promise.
What blows me away about Propel is that they've done this much good without really getting a ton of buy-in from government. "Trying to get a contract with the federal government seemed like it would be time-consuming and arduous, so we figured let's just try to solve a problem for people and if we make it good enough, users will find us."
Imagine if Jimmy and Propel were not just helping people navigate through food stamps but also healthcare and retirement benefits? "We would honestly do it for free. I mean, we're not charging anyone now and we've built a profitable business because we're touching so many people's lives and there's so much inefficiency. We'd save governments millions, even billions." Still, originally Jimmy had to overcome a lot of skepticism from investors. "They hadn't had much direct experience with this, so they had a hard time reaching conviction. Eventually we found investors who actually understood the benefits system and how many people it affects."
I asked Jimmy what we could do to help Propel grow. "Obviously, if you know someone who's using food stamps, send them our way so we can help make their lives better. We'd like to start working with individual states on their programs too. We'd save them all money - we are just scratching the surface of what we can do."
If you know me, you know that poverty is the problem I want to see solved the most. I'm working on improving our political system, but we know that's a long-term approach that's going to have fits and starts. Jimmy didn't wait, and millions of people are better off for it today. It's the kind of approach that deserves a lot more attention, because we might be able to improve people's lives without waiting for D.C.
For my interview with Jimmy, click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/16690904-e858-4f33-bc14-156871afbbaf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). To see what Forward is doing in your state, click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/12d0c145-709e-4649-ac58-a249a183ed8f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). We will be launching something soon that we hope eventually makes as big a difference as Propel - it's a very high bar. Also, we are having an [Offline Party](https://substack.com/redirect/ed875073-d401-4193-8725-62617a8dff0b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on Thursday in LA so if you're available and in the area come join us!
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b4eb9305-58ea-41cf-b902-d19e48f65e2d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

name: Kirk Cameron
subject: The #1 thing you can do today to stop the Hollywood groomers
body:
Please -- for the sake of all our families

| | |
---
| | Dear Friend, Please -- for the sake of all our families -- take it from me as someone who's lived and worked in Hollywood my whole life... You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children.You may remember shows with wholesome messages like Father Knows Best, Marcus Welby, M.D., and even Dennis the Menace.But today when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the Pride Parade on _Blue's Clues_...
---
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zjgmn979me69h62ntu2821rv33mcu2eolacue4b0_rp22sh2s8i6epb2clg69pr2c9h2bs)
---
| | Or Sesame Street using YOUR tax-dollars to normalize same-sex parents and men in dresses.And the result is an upside-down world where children lecture YOU about what you can and cannot say... ...and they try to "educate" YOU about your "old-fashioned" and "mean" ideas.Today you have a very special chance to stop the Hollywood groomers who are turning our children and grandchildren against us and the Godly values we love. [**Today, I'm inviting you to support the very first children’s television show that actively fights back against the groomers in Hollywood AND reinforces the traditional biblical values that made America great.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z9pg3ojlldigvivt0iq8v10eernodpct8c1fq5ag_rp22sh2s8i6epb2clg69pr2c9h2bs) And it’s only possible with the help of grandparents and seniors like you. Introducing:_ Iggy and Mr. Kirk_.This ground-breaking live-action television show captivates children... ...but more importantly, it builds their character at the same time. Imagine a children’s show that leaves your child or grandchild with a greater sense of right and wrong and an understanding of the truth. Courage, honesty, discernment, forgiveness, empathy, all taught through a biblical and traditional lens. Imagine a children’s show that brings families together instead of driving them apart. Now imagine YOU are the one who makes that show possible. **[Because I can’t beat the Hollywood groomers without your help.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z0noqg4mf1vaicqp4r4qr8b15691lpp6stea8aa0_rp22sh2s8i6epb2clg69pr2c9h2bs)** Millions of families are already watching Season One of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ on our streaming service BRAVE+, YouTube, and on DVD. And the reviews from parents, grandparents, and kids are incredible. One Mom of three little girls told us: “Our family was struggling through a pretty difficult day. But I turned on _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ and that was exactly what my heart needed as much as theirs.” It’s amazing that your simple response to this letter can make that kind of difference for a family. Each episode costs around $70,000 to produce. I know that’s a lot of money but it’s actually a fraction of what it would cost if we made our show with a major Hollywood studio. And obviously we won’t work through Hollywood. Hollywood has done quite enough already to destroy American culture and hijack our children. We parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens must take over to rebuild a thriving, healthy, GOOD and GODLY culture. [**That’s why I’m asking you to help us finish Season Two of**** _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_****by giving a gift of $35, $50, $100, $250, $500, $1,000 or more to help us reach our fundraising goal of $70,000 per episode.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zqbjlg94s7euarcqt0t15h98mm1ena1arejcc8qo_rp22sh2s8i6epb2clg69pr2c9h2bs)**** Whether you can send $35, $25,000, anything in between, or anything higher or lower – please know that gifts of EVERY size are needed and greatly appreciated. As a special “Thank You” for your **[gift of $50 or more](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zcp7eoooo1bspmcimrhvisat0t6ap528lap7mh18_rp22sh2s8i6epb2clg69pr2c9h2bs)** , I’ll send you Season One of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ for your family to enjoy FREE on DVD. subject: Today you have a very special chance to stop the Hollywood corruption
body:

Now we’re just $8,497 shy of our digital fundraising goal. We rely on your support to keep our programs running in every corner of the state, boosting local democratic parties, and engaging voters year-round. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | — NV Dems The Nevada State Democratic Party fights for progress in the Silver State.
