name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: URGENT ACTION: DOGE cuts in Trouble
body:
Tell the Senate to pass the Rescissions Act NOW!
| | | |
---
|
---
| Patriot-

---
| **** **** The Rescissions Act of 2025, which would make the DOGE cuts permanent, is in trouble thanks to the usual squishy Senate Republicans. The act will expire on July 18th, and the Republican-led Senate has yet to bring this bill to the floor for a vote.
We need you to act _right now_ and use our [action page](https://qcgp-cmpzourl.maillist-manage.com/click/1edb9794ac9a85be/1edb9794ac99b8a6) to tell your senator that it's time to use their position to do the will of the people, instead of working for the special interest groups and lobbyists who want to continue to siphon American tax dollars without an ounce of accountability.
**You must act _now_ and [tell your senators](https://qcgp-cmpzourl.maillist-manage.com/click/1edb9794ac9a85be/1edb9794ac99b8a8 "https://action.teapartypatriots.org/petition/demand-action-cd") that they need to pass this bill immediately!**

__ **__****__****__****__** __ **__** **** ************
---
| | [ TAKE ACTION ](https://qcgp-cmpzourl.maillist-manage.com/click/1edb9794ac9a85be/1edb9794ac99b8aa)
---
| **** ******************** ******If the Senate does not pass it before the deadline with enough time for the House to pass it again if the Senate makes any changes, President Trump will be FORCED TO SPEND THE MONEY that DOGE identified as waste, fraud, and abuse.****
****_Put the pressure on the Senate NOW!_** In liberty,Jenny Beth Martin __ **__****__****__****__** __ **__** **** ************
---

body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23209%2E4449941%2EOu4VIT) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23209%2E4449941%2EOu4VIT) | [**WOAH!!**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/64165?t=3&akid=23209%2E4449941%2EOu4VIT)

Senators Amy Klobuchar and Chuck Schumer are trying to pass an AMAZING Constitutional Amendment...

[**It would END the influence of Dark Money and prevent Billionaires like Elon Musk from rigging elections for Republicans!**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/64165?t=4&akid=23209%2E4449941%2EOu4VIT)

_This is so awesome!!_

[**_We need EVERY committed Democrat reading this to endorse Klobuchar and Schumer 's bill to hit 25,OOO signatures before 10:00 AM tomorrow >>_**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/64166?t=5&akid=23209%2E4449941%2EOu4VIT) | []()
---|---
| [SIGN YOUR NAME ->](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/64165?t=6&akid=23209%2E4449941%2EOu4VIT)
---
Amy Klobuchar and Chuck Schumer's plan is vitally important.

Here's what it would do:

**FIRST:** It would overturn _Citizens United_ - which has ERODED our democracy for fifteen years.

**SECOND:** It would prevent Super PACs and Dark Money donors from flooding undisclosed funds into our elections.

**FINALLY:** It would stop Billionaires from EVER attempting to buy our elections again!!

This would be groundbreaking.

And it would give Democrats a HUGE opportunity to win the Midterms and take back our Majority in 2026 - fair and square!

[**_If you 're on board with Klobuchar and Schumer's Constitutional Amendment, please join 24,999 other good Democrats in signing our petition to give them the momentum they need!_**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/64165?t=7&akid=23209%2E4449941%2EOu4VIT)
| [SIGN YOUR NAME ->](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/64165?t=8&akid=23209%2E4449941%2EOu4VIT)

name: James O'Keefe
subject: I’ve Risked Everything for the Truth– Will You Stand with Me?
body:
There have been nights when I’ve questioned if it’s worth it. The legal bills, the threats, the constant pressure—it takes a toll. I’ve sacrificed relationships, financial security, and my own safety. But then I think of the lives we’ve touched. Like the voters who saw our exposés on election and Voter ID fraud, leading to new laws in multiple states.

| [****](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zjl79qr5n8suekql40u9abjfk34nbtoqhsfqv0k0_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)
[****](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zl0d3shiqh0fn4khh2uvu4k8vqvu7nghcr8nn718_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs) Dear Patriot,

I'm James O'Keefe, and I'm writing to you not just as a journalist, but as someone who's poured his heart, soul, and life into a mission that's bigger than me.

For over 15 years, I've fought to expose the truth--about corruption, fraud, and the powerful who think they can hide in the shadows. It's been a journey of sacrifice, risk, and relentless determination, and I'm asking you to [**join me in this fight by supporting**O 'Keefe Media Group****](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zili728no2qirvhn2g2r6qb6a0lmo4u98121pqgo_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs).

Picture me in 2009, a kid from Westwood, New Jersey, with nothing but a cheap camera and a fire in my belly.

I grew up in a family that taught me to work hard and stand for what's right, but I had no money, no connections, just a belief that the American people deserved to know what was happening behind closed doors.

I maxed out credit cards to buy plane tickets and equipment, [**racking up $50,000 in debt for one investigation alone --exposing NPR's bias in 2011**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zgelhrkl2udo3dmuuj57nmeukha1m38aoc29oo90_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs).

I'd lie awake at night, wondering how I'd pay it off, but I kept going because I knew the truth was worth it.

A few years before that in 2009, I got my big break. I went undercover at ACORN, a group funded by your tax dollars. Posing as a pimp and with an undercover journalist posing as a prostitute, we caught employees on hidden camera offering advice on dodging taxes and running illegal operations. The videos hit like a thunderbolt--**Congress cut ACORN 's funding, and the organization collapsed**.

It was proof that one person could change the game. But it also painted a target on my back. The powerful don't like being exposed**, and they 've been coming for me ever since**.

**[**Will you help me keep exposing the truth?**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zmpfa2akd2nhkf9gmk8irpgkde5orr2hb5rpgfl0_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)**

I need to raise **$28,400 as soon as possible** to fund our July operations, cover travel for our next sting, and pay our team. [**Your gift of $25, $50, or $100 will keep our cameras rolling and our mission alive. Donate now.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zt1uroffjsdf2bnii8bsonsppp0ji45uh1amumco_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)

In 2010, I faced one of my darkest moments.

I was arrested for entering Senator Mary Landrieu's office, investigating claims she was ignoring constituents during the healthcare debate. Despite showing my ID went I entered the building, I was still charged for entering a federal building under false pretenses.

The government slapped me with felony charges, and I spent a night in a jail cell, [**staring at the walls, wondering if my dream was over.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z2ugulctnut614eh5gu17m48a6tm9g5461fq09c8_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)

I was 25, facing years in prison, and the media painted me as a villain. But I thought of the people counting on me--the Americans who deserved to know the truth--and I refused to give up.

I pleaded guilty to a misdemeanor, got three years of probation, 100 hours of community service, and a $1,500 fine. **Even on probation, I didn 't stop**. I built a team of citizen journalists and kept digging, exposing voter fraud and media bias while under the government's thumb.

That resilience paid off. In 2011, we released videos that forced NPR's CEO and executives to resign, revealing their bias to the world.

In 2014, I crossed the Rio Grande dressed as Osama bin Laden to expose border security failures, sparking a U.S. Senate investigation. My mentor, Andrew Breitbart, called me "one of the great journalists," and **Rush Limbaugh said our NPR sting was a "grand slam."**

But every victory came with a cost--more lawsuits, more threats, more attempts to silence me.

**[**Your support can keep this fight alive**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z3aobijpcirjjt33t0hkstv92ta802bugfc0v6no_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)****.** We're the only ones exposing the bureaucrats undermining our Republic, and we need **$28,400 by in days** to continue. [**A donation of $50 or $200 will fund our next investigation and protect our work from activist lawsuits**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z5a2kqs73hnjsjnnl70s6vstjk0cdsrbnofchjrg_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs).

The hardest blow came in 2021.

**I woke up to the FBI pounding on my door in Mamaroneck, New York, raiding my home over a diary allegedly belonging to President Biden 's daughter. **

They took my phones, my computers, everything--trying to scare me into submission.

I stood in my apartment, watching them tear through my life, and felt the weight of their power.

But I also felt a fire inside me.

If they were coming this hard, it meant I was hitting a nerve.

I refused to back down, speaking out on Fox News with Sean Hannity, declaring, "This is an attack on the First Amendment." That moment strengthened my resolve to keep fighting, no matter the odds.

The lawsuits have been relentless.

From ACORN employees to activist attorneys, I've faced legal battles that would break most people. Even my own organization, Project Veritas, sued me in 2023, alleging financial mismanagement after I founded O'Keefe Media Group.

They tried to bury me, but I kept going, **because the truth is bigger than any one fight.**

**[**I need you now more than ever**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z0bhntgi8m9gsqalfsvbtl5sav86fshjk284cpbo_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)****.** Summer donations are down, and [**we must raise**$28,400 within days** to keep exposing corruption. **](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zk33bb0fp6s3rdli1a8c5rfiqt0p4hljmmpnee18_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)

[**Your gift of $25 or $75 will help us train citizen journalists and fight back against those trying to silence us. Donate today.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zi7lr3j1d5a5qb71uknhiadbj7ladu7ndoib63kg_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)

There have been nights when I've questioned if it's worth it.

The legal bills, the threats, the constant pressure--it takes a toll.

I've sacrificed relationships, financial security, and my own safety.

But then I think of the lives we've touched. Like the voters who saw our exposes on election and Voter ID fraud, leading to new laws in multiple states.

Or the families who benefited when we exposed Medicaid incompetence, forcing retraining. Every time I feel like giving up, I remember why I started: **because the American people deserve a government that serves them, not one that hides behind lies.**

With **O 'Keefe Media Group**, I'm building a legacy.

We're not just reporting news--we're training an army of citizen journalists to carry the torch. Our recent work has exposed media cover-ups, government waste, and corporate greed, sparking firings and policy reviews. But we're not done.

The bureaucrats undermining President Trump's agenda, wasting your tax dollars, and pushing their own agendas are still out there. **We 're the only ones holding them accountable with hidden cameras and fearless journalism.**

**[**Will you stand with me?**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z468oq6ngr6bvd9hakt6sibffebjp3loq5hkmcqo_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)**

[**We need**$28,400 within days** to keep going. **](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zgasu71k63hn59rciuihd93d7vo057kvqr22glqo_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)

[**Your donation of $50, $100, or $500 is a direct blow against corruption, ensuring the truth reaches millions.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z4hfjd9qd3nt012566psh6t9ulb1dhp8d0st6qlg_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)

I'm not an activist. I'm a journalist, driven by an obsession to uncover what the powerful want hidden.

The mainstream media won't do it--they're too busy protecting the elites.

But I've never backed down, and I never will. I've risked everything for this mission, and I'm asking you to join me.

Your support isn't just a donation--[**it 's a lifeline**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zi40i21kbjnm8bona7l702he8e001704rpla4ne0_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs).

It's a statement that you believe in truth, transparency, **and the fight for our Republic**.

Please, if my story moves you, if you believe in what we're doing, [**donate today**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zq3pkce14ao70aiqin0fsqtaccggq910rg5ugei8_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs). [**We 're down to the wire, and every dollar counts. **](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zjeem0fbsa92le79oiogo8v3c1m1eguu5ijpu0p0_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)

Together, we can keep **exposing the truth and holding the powerful accountable**.

Thanks for standing with me,
James O’Keefe
Founder and CEO
O'Keefe Media Group

---
[****](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zg8pgrqvne0fqjoof1qv9ihanlkb0kjdqu5sikso_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)
---

Pop Acta Media | GOP Presidential
---

_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zg807norq92cn7ij0q1lvevkj1ein5cbv7pkgvso_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph238h5e8he485he5o75pt7kuo72ttle5pqedtge1p7adjkm4qj7cbkmgor79hnmmrqastmelp3cthieip3adt7skqu2c3neior3d5medjm05plmgqb3ct66uqr695lshgm6p72cph649fg)_ | _[manage data preferences](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z7mr452ume7p2dchup98cg5cev0u5ju38bd8paho_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli64ob4cph2bs)_

body:
| | | **** ******** **** **** **** **** **Measles is back – with a vengeance.** **[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtHtWNDtXR0WReOmqZcCOXOd0KsCyQ6NLH2vKJToAIQspAbPSpPuaj1jgwGWpjiBAgePscIpYXv9ygB3e1J1-ofldDfPIzOIMQlLP18Jn8YfCKagP_1GLFkuic09LW8HvF2GF1OFbeM4PrvWEfmy3H7JPwCoUNmYwh4nOPVqitGJ3UjP0ksdwOvh2X4xxG5duzAOgoLFYG4mhYQ_Jy8C-8YKZHxJwrp_wmFf55uvBX4cf6jBy3eza2yZTbFI1027P5hSVxgGMOMjiByJbgsbFWBlcmgDsi8iqGXAVAOj_xLqUyo1VABuRO5cgdhFEYqOMsc6fSUeafLW8pMF9ZitSaJ/4i6/uetjbIP5QOi5Y3zKkzaAoQ/h0/h001.1IVtlHBNWGleBasbDDwZoqOWPXUiF9IvYbr0bqj7q1w)**[**Chip in to help Able Dems run ads against Republicans who are attacking public health and life-saving vaccines!**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtHtWNDtXR0WReOmqZcCOXOd0KsCyQ6NLH2vKJToAIQshfxOMHBXLnsHYw0j60qSdRgDiMik5t7pSMY0qu0aw9THQ9bGsQR8G2U9-_AZkLFcQ0Uk7awXC_sOYv9qqkK6H-eB38jRmm5Smsbp7pREEyCKoRMHyv1hQiyLOtPLGR3lpV3cDbDrmPEJtxTE6z0DgHD1a9UuLio3r4i_DnTSa0VgAjaFZQttK_bZ-iSnMRgEHg2XqEc_AItae0HHfMDY172AGs3sZgz4zA-f8ztrtdYfTK3UjB2vk4OMVRgcyUYuzeMeq0U0OA6A0SLskgwKx8hHWqVv_Dy-elevAL1hzge/4i6/uetjbIP5QOi5Y3zKkzaAoQ/h1/h001.-NsI_HK8f6EepqSmheiQYkw6I9jIhoPXcox7bQnJY1c)
* * *
Trump’s Secretary of Health and Human Services and conspiracy quack RFK Jr., has spread deadly misinformation which has caused vaccine hesitancy _and the resurgence of measles._ The disease which was declared eradicated in the 1960s thanks to the historic success of a national vaccination program is back with a vengeance. In 2000 there were 86 measles cases in the US. Today, the U.S. has recorded 1,281 measles cases, with 155 hospitalizations and three deaths, including two unvaccinated children. clean_body: | | | ** **** Measles is back – with a vengeance. Trump’s Secretary of Health and Human Services and conspiracy quack RFK Jr., has spread deadly misinformation which has caused vaccine hesitancy and the resurgence of measles. The disease which was declared eradicated in the 1960s thanks to the historic success of a national vaccination program is back with a vengeance. In 2000 there were 86 measles cases in the US. Today, the U.S. has recorded 1,281 measles cases, with 155 hospitalizations and three deaths, including two unvaccinated children. This marks the first measles-related deaths in a decade, highlighting a concerning rise in infections. The Trump regime is not making America healthy again! **If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. ** A decline in measles vaccination rates could lead to 11.1 million infections over 25 years, reviving other preventable diseases. The resulting illnesses and deaths would be "absolutely devastating," per a study coauthored by Peter Hotez, dean for the National School of Tropical Medicine at Baylor College of Medicine. name: James O'Keefe
subject: I’ve Risked Everything for the Truth– Will You Stand with Me?
body:
There have been nights when I’ve questioned if it’s worth it. The legal bills, the threats, the constant pressure—it takes a toll. I’ve sacrificed relationships, financial security, and my own safety. But then I think of the lives we’ve touched. Like the voters who saw our exposés on election and Voter ID fraud, leading to new laws in multiple states.

| [****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zhomh3ueoqh8kde0l4o0qtul226ohsqpi4f2vle0_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs)
[****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zik860702vhgn2eb4l0svquu2b5l3oab9g8mqs9g_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs) Dear Patriot,

I'm James O'Keefe, and I'm writing to you not just as a journalist, but as someone who's poured his heart, soul, and life into a mission that's bigger than me.

For over 15 years, I've fought to expose the truth--about corruption, fraud, and the powerful who think they can hide in the shadows. It's been a journey of sacrifice, risk, and relentless determination, and I'm asking you to [**join me in this fight by supporting**O 'Keefe Media Group****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zn9i9bhv44vf1em938cop08j1dcviph2kjue0b80_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs).

Picture me in 2009, a kid from Westwood, New Jersey, with nothing but a cheap camera and a fire in my belly.

I grew up in a family that taught me to work hard and stand for what's right, but I had no money, no connections, just a belief that the American people deserved to know what was happening behind closed doors.

I maxed out credit cards to buy plane tickets and equipment, [**racking up $50,000 in debt for one investigation alone --exposing NPR's bias in 2011**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zpuhue2u74etkc6n2de04efjljg5abhqm2rt1cqo_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs).

I'd lie awake at night, wondering how I'd pay it off, but I kept going because I knew the truth was worth it.

A few years before that in 2009, I got my big break. I went undercover at ACORN, a group funded by your tax dollars. Posing as a pimp and with an undercover journalist posing as a prostitute, we caught employees on hidden camera offering advice on dodging taxes and running illegal operations. The videos hit like a thunderbolt--**Congress cut ACORN 's funding, and the organization collapsed**.

It was proof that one person could change the game. But it also painted a target on my back. The powerful don't like being exposed**, and they 've been coming for me ever since**.

**[**Will you help me keep exposing the truth?**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zio4kmtn4jsdg6al8l92k1lqa6bht8ht7e3vckug_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs)**

I need to raise **$28,400 as soon as possible** to fund our July operations, cover travel for our next sting, and pay our team. [**Your gift of $25, $50, or $100 will keep our cameras rolling and our mission alive. Donate now.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zjg21khejnfr9u8b3a5to8q97hcm15gjesdu0948_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs)

In 2010, I faced one of my darkest moments.

I was arrested for entering Senator Mary Landrieu's office, investigating claims she was ignoring constituents during the healthcare debate. Despite showing my ID went I entered the building, I was still charged for entering a federal building under false pretenses.

The government slapped me with felony charges, and I spent a night in a jail cell, [**staring at the walls, wondering if my dream was over.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zki1n56lqqv5kldi71rgs1vmurqll7j26ttsddi0_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs)

I was 25, facing years in prison, and the media painted me as a villain. But I thought of the people counting on me--the Americans who deserved to know the truth--and I refused to give up.

I pleaded guilty to a misdemeanor, got three years of probation, 100 hours of community service, and a $1,500 fine. **Even on probation, I didn 't stop**. I built a team of citizen journalists and kept digging, exposing voter fraud and media bias while under the government's thumb.

That resilience paid off. In 2011, we released videos that forced NPR's CEO and executives to resign, revealing their bias to the world.

In 2014, I crossed the Rio Grande dressed as Osama bin Laden to expose border security failures, sparking a U.S. Senate investigation. My mentor, Andrew Breitbart, called me "one of the great journalists," and **Rush Limbaugh said our NPR sting was a "grand slam."**

But every victory came with a cost--more lawsuits, more threats, more attempts to silence me.

**[**Your support can keep this fight alive**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zv21dsgs2sm4i52oq4o6u83unsonp96sucr5til8_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs)****.** We're the only ones exposing the bureaucrats undermining our Republic, and we need **$28,400 by in days** to continue. [**A donation of $50 or $200 will fund our next investigation and protect our work from activist lawsuits**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zmgjkb7koo8j531hheqslmhkqtouhlh1hpf8pr1g_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs).

The hardest blow came in 2021.

**I woke up to the FBI pounding on my door in Mamaroneck, New York, raiding my home over a diary allegedly belonging to President Biden 's daughter. **

They took my phones, my computers, everything--trying to scare me into submission.

I stood in my apartment, watching them tear through my life, and felt the weight of their power.

But I also felt a fire inside me.

If they were coming this hard, it meant I was hitting a nerve.

I refused to back down, speaking out on Fox News with Sean Hannity, declaring, "This is an attack on the First Amendment." That moment strengthened my resolve to keep fighting, no matter the odds.

The lawsuits have been relentless.

From ACORN employees to activist attorneys, I've faced legal battles that would break most people. Even my own organization, Project Veritas, sued me in 2023, alleging financial mismanagement after I founded O'Keefe Media Group.

They tried to bury me, but I kept going, **because the truth is bigger than any one fight.**

**[**I need you now more than ever**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zlfe09akr1jvpl1pk987nfrbislte5nv6dffrdsg_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs)****.** Summer donations are down, and [**we must raise**$28,400 within days** to keep exposing corruption. **](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z1mamlia0o6cm4vgqksf762fgi0l6qbe9gl09t38_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs)

[**Your gift of $25 or $75 will help us train citizen journalists and fight back against those trying to silence us. Donate today.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zoke7a4m0blv2skom1ke72vg11ahsjfciim723ho_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs)

There have been nights when I've questioned if it's worth it.

The legal bills, the threats, the constant pressure--it takes a toll.

I've sacrificed relationships, financial security, and my own safety.

But then I think of the lives we've touched. Like the voters who saw our exposes on election and Voter ID fraud, leading to new laws in multiple states.

Or the families who benefited when we exposed Medicaid incompetence, forcing retraining. Every time I feel like giving up, I remember why I started: **because the American people deserve a government that serves them, not one that hides behind lies.**

With **O 'Keefe Media Group**, I'm building a legacy.

We're not just reporting news--we're training an army of citizen journalists to carry the torch. Our recent work has exposed media cover-ups, government waste, and corporate greed, sparking firings and policy reviews. But we're not done.

The bureaucrats undermining President Trump's agenda, wasting your tax dollars, and pushing their own agendas are still out there. **We 're the only ones holding them accountable with hidden cameras and fearless journalism.**

**[**Will you stand with me?**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zemvjc4li8n4ct3fkd12077khc1q25gu5u26nl3o_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs)**

[**We need**$28,400 within days** to keep going. **](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zrcr3pbbnuif7j4gue67lv6v7d0rp4k37dqa8rp0_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs)

[**Your donation of $50, $100, or $500 is a direct blow against corruption, ensuring the truth reaches millions.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2ze24o2m3sgcssoa4shgg0faavi7eo1openavstco_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs)

I'm not an activist. I'm a journalist, driven by an obsession to uncover what the powerful want hidden.

The mainstream media won't do it--they're too busy protecting the elites.

But I've never backed down, and I never will. I've risked everything for this mission, and I'm asking you to join me.

Your support isn't just a donation--[**it 's a lifeline**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zng6uvpql905f8qjdebieh2p8aanol103nf2i6kg_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs).

It's a statement that you believe in truth, transparency, **and the fight for our Republic**.

Please, if my story moves you, if you believe in what we're doing, [**donate today**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zk5opanc3q6is48r25mq8jou9c0dpa6jtg1gtu78_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs). [**We 're down to the wire, and every dollar counts. **](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z4dfubpgglgt4io8jm85ekjl9r7c7arkpp60uk1g_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cli6cpj2cth2bs)

Together, we can keep **exposing the truth and holding the powerful accountable**.

Thanks for standing with me,
James O’Keefe
Founder and CEO
O'Keefe Media Group

Peter,

This is Coach Tuberville, and when I coached football, I told my players you can’t win a game if you don’t play defense.

Right now, Alabama’s defense is breaking down, and crime is running up the score.

Thanks to Joe Biden and Kamala Harris leaving our country broken and falling apart, too many of our neighborhoods are falling behind due to drug trafficking and violent crime. **We can’t continue to let these criminals take the field unchallenged.**

As Governor, I promise to work with President Trump to put the safety of Alabamians FIRST.

But, if I’m going to build a game plan to turn this around, I need to hear straight from the folks living it.

**[That’s why I’m asking you to fill out this quick Crime and Safety Survey.](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=decea52fcc5846b78e97038803e25c18&_e=cMudpYqxOX2LBK2ap31AOUmv7qt1ysw7yaf9d8NsgnMkf_0Ei6YH9BoAGPA2dqgCtKPQv_GJ3P8em63sqneM2suD4azlyzeofgYHVef2sv8yy2mss0xljlZW9Z2Zh4Oh_GvlP4vwnT9eV0LbBAYWIeDF_uuf1mng8Yg5lVT2A1UvJyvAjIUwCGUOXz-Pu8tjYAzNEfbuN08mL27lIH0diUNnMmw0j54L6hFq-ud5swXgtEMMf-9boM2lq6QeJdU4mw-AAyufOPr0ea68nkntGqAh_aFgxdSvgCc2pVSBrAPBxvN6k7jVAT9XVJD8ZN7W29X5KmZgxQ9WdsKGFDlQF-FrUXOJE1Q87w0tPn8wUb-sk_qykPUDbrJv4Vwkf1QvteqXJDr8AXQZGq9N_MvFXCOt7lKrc-v9wSTR7XVnXaoJ3zGbiO2ctmi8Xa04daXVAnx4SGk-BFXs9cCRfShXeq6eK9u2Myb_r9ybeBnR9ZxMhAB3egRoexRVZh77S9b7BmhykIXBomw6wQQsd__VYLCwtot1q9vfegy1miT8mgyZ47DB2tpcp_YH9ANO2IDD)**

_**When you’re the coach, you don’t guess. You listen to your team, you study the film, and you build a plan that wins.**_

That’s exactly how I’m going to tackle crime in Alabama, but I need your voice in the huddle.

**[Let’s fix this together.](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=decea52fcc5846b78e97038803e25c18&_e=cMudpYqxOX2LBK2ap31AOUmv7qt1ysw7yaf9d8NsgnMkf_0Ei6YH9BoAGPA2dqgCtKPQv_GJ3P8em63sqneM2suD4azlyzeofgYHVef2sv8yy2mss0xljlZW9Z2Zh4Oh_GvlP4vwnT9eV0LbBAYWIeDF_uuf1mng8Yg5lVT2A1UvJyvAjIUwCGUOXz-Pu8tjOibwDj1XE7y28sGgfNjmUgm8XcuEbtW8C0SEM2o8lviL-4mxNv14HyY173f55iVUYJAV2Yx0LL5h5skQyBMIYthnQIfAsPC8ZU3rSXqJdh4rmM4sv7UB9_25ZWRmIXJsrz6jEzZnNEVcppDMaFoJCv-onTN_5iX0rAWzv_tgHDTRIkzGCYD0REUFH8anGNncXLVcC0Fdr2oVTXMps9j3zg3entQOpyxCft8ZR7VNSY94V9Phr9Na6dUxp2HC540uewSd8pxmikjwPLJf4KkNxCsZfNPPZIDxr-riwUQ9GkGwgufjkQ5JFyvPZgEXTH1eeC60jBu9pKpZ86CEsTrl0p2wNbnJNDYgX667N6GF-bUUnkvJ-LqzCU6YK1a1dS_7)**

Coach Tuberville

| [](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=decea52fcc5846b78e97038803e25c18&_e=cMudpYqxOX2LBK2ap31AOUmv7qt1ysw7yaf9d8NsgnMkf_0Ei6YH9BoAGPA2dqgCtKPQv_GJ3P8em63sqneM2suD4azlyzeofgYHVef2sv8yy2mss0xljlZW9Z2Zh4Oh_GvlP4vwnT9eV0LbBAYWIeDF_uuf1mng8Yg5lVT2A1UvJyvAjIUwCGUOXz-Pu8tj0ETpe9DQZFov0d_9ZZQJiUKJbcj-5Ihc9WdJIJSOLXZb3lNGYtVF2su7xZoNdzJiQQSlDvD3J7jz6ldxAWS9ayh1DTl6M3cD6OL9EM0TgAGWSmAA4cehtBMenAgEEM_EPtuKeABCt4p_4rVpQ36t2ioo-_XnllIsMdEzSxogcpQ1b0Krom8F0NOIXcF6bxQS4b2Qcu3mujNji5cUJC1XT-mwaUNKpc2CS7Ma3-eUd3JJwHmJ3MO6pH3F-9H4W3qOZ7xaglopkRnNNYEyZR8jtJg7U8igxE1m3-1-3r6-fUirjLTsekD7ZI0UsciV6H0tuILV1uCgt9SsHWAj-iVUd3T_G6QsqiZb9CZ9-iOF4-4x9WBJDSJFlClc_lcn7YTy)

Meet Coach Tuberville:

**Coach Tuberville was born in Camden, Arkansas, the third of three children of Olive and Charles Tuberville. College Football’s 2004 Coach of the Year and two-time SEC Coach of the Year, Tommy Tuberville is most often recognized for his success on the field. But it is the legacy of his father, a highly decorated WWII veteran and Purple Heart recipient, that motivates Coach Tuberville to give back to the brave men and women of the United States Armed Forces.

In 1989 Coach Tuberville met his future wife, Suzanne Fette, at the Sugar Bowl in New Orleans. They were married in 1991 and have two boys, Tucker and Troy, who continue to make their dad proud.

In his career as a College Football Coach, Tommy started a long and successful career at the University of Miami, starting as a graduate assistant and rising to the Hurricanes' defensive coordinator. He led the Canes to three AP national championships and coached future Hall of Famers Ray Lewis and Warren Sapp, as well as Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson.

In 1998, Coach Tuberville was announced as the next head coach for the Auburn Tigers. Tuberville’s time at Auburn was a success by any measure, as the Tigers captured eight consecutive bowl appearances, including five New Year’s Day bowl appearances, five SEC Western Division titles, one SEC Championship, and a 13-0 season in 2004, after which Coach Tuberville was once again named the AP’s SEC Coach of the Year. For his efforts during the perfect 2004 season, Tuberville would be named Paul “Bear” Bryant Coach of the Year, Associated Press Coach of the Year, Walter Camp Coach of the Year, Sporting News Coach of the Year, and AFCA Coach of the Year, among others.

_In 2020, Coach Tuberville became Senator Tuberville, winning his election to represent all Alabamians in the United States Senate with the full support of President Trump. Since then, he's supported President Trump's America-First policies while fighting off the Radical Left. In his first term as Senator, Tommy was one of eight Republicans to vote against certifying the 2020 election, has been a strong pro-life advocate, and called out Democrats for being pro-crime.

Now, Coach Tuberville is running for Governor so he can continue representing Alabamians and Americans in his homestate of Alabama. The Radical Left thinks they can flip Alabama blue and turn it into the California of the South. Can he count on your support? _

** [**HELP COACH TUBERVILLE**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=decea52fcc5846b78e97038803e25c18&_e=cMudpYqxOX2LBK2ap31AOUmv7qt1ysw7yaf9d8NsgnMkf_0Ei6YH9BoAGPA2dqgCtKPQv_GJ3P8em63sqneM2suD4azlyzeofgYHVef2sv8yy2mss0xljlZW9Z2Zh4Oh_GvlP4vwnT9eV0LbBAYWIeDF_uuf1mng8Yg5lVT2A1UvJyvAjIUwCGUOXz-Pu8tjikWcC-tj0YpCMjWpvfefiLAawKbplBzwGo3YmSefs6PFbN6y_5mNimsepWJiUeFYA-b3Oqy0ijUzPYYDTvTf9FxyizlgyykfVuEczNfy90E-QOt8i0bGe-qKd7JmPpv7DSOtFJR24krrHYrBD3EC1YO62UA6OVTFfsUOU3Yx8d9mGa9KeaRaMi8xKX_QsNlF0yAfaRXyqu42FWOxIzIQRJanqW9ONe9dkt-K3WnXK6eIHMKWGtiqjsXgt1cvgCxD9eh9sZiTg0m25Y-TqSyvB6VZ1QAM4Kd7XCPqDctHbynqOE7m3utcb-VbcYUcbdvoEvyg2aipDTP6lA8lMRyK6HwZpUqpa6n1C9Bt6ltXqODf72dAED6vWdi__Nx8_ein)

You listen to your team, you study the film, and you build a plan that wins. That’s exactly how I’m going to tackle crime in Alabama, but I need your voice in the huddle. Coach Tuberville | Meet Coach Tuberville: Coach Tuberville was born in Camden, Arkansas, the third of three children of Olive and Charles Tuberville. College Football’s 2004 Coach of the Year and two-time SEC Coach of the Year, Tommy Tuberville is most often recognized for his success on the field. But it is the legacy of his father, a highly decorated WWII veteran and Purple Heart recipient, that motivates Coach Tuberville to give back to the brave men and women of the United States Armed Forces. In 1989 Coach Tuberville met his future wife, Suzanne Fette, at the Sugar Bowl in New Orleans. They were married in 1991 and have two boys, Tucker and Troy, who continue to make their dad proud. In his career as a College Football Coach, Tommy started a long and successful career at the University of Miami, starting as a graduate assistant and rising to the Hurricanes' defensive coordinator. He led the Canes to three AP national championships and coached future Hall of Famers Ray Lewis and Warren Sapp, as well as Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson. In 1998, Coach Tuberville was announced as the next head coach for the Auburn Tigers. Tuberville’s time at Auburn was a success by any measure, as the Tigers captured eight consecutive bowl appearances, including five New Year’s Day bowl appearances, five SEC Western Division titles, one SEC Championship, and a 13-0 season in 2004, after which Coach Tuberville was once again named the AP’s SEC Coach of the Year. For his efforts during the perfect 2004 season, Tuberville would be named Paul “Bear” Bryant Coach of the Year, Associated Press Coach of the Year, Walter Camp Coach of the Year, Sporting News Coach of the Year, and AFCA Coach of the Year, among others. In 2020, Coach Tuberville became Senator Tuberville, winning his election to represent all Alabamians in the United States Senate with the full support of President Trump. Since then, he's supported President Trump's America-First policies while fighting off the Radical Left. In his first term as Senator, Tommy was one of eight Republicans to vote against certifying the 2020 election, has been a strong pro-life advocate, and called out Democrats for being pro-crime. Now, Coach Tuberville is running for Governor so he can continue representing Alabamians and Americans in his homestate of Alabama. The Radical Left thinks they can flip Alabama blue and turn it into the California of the South. Can he count on your support? PAID FOR BY TUBERVILLE FOR GOVERNOR, INC PO Box 43124 Birmingham, AL 35243 Copyright (C) 2025 Tuberville for Governor, Inc, All rights reserved.

name: Josh Weil for U.S. Senate
subject: MAGA made this mess
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Weil, Democrat for US Senate
body:
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/387153444E713877456653376A556574376F314841345859327A4D665362374C42586753794B64635252546F6551413647436E7356536243345157355952664A/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-06-18%20at%2011.41.25%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Friend, this morning’s headline says it all:
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/365946736B655477776A61635339776B72554C784F4F5873343058356D59524F5968356A6472766D44714A4D2F654F6E394C763543626E71457638343467554B/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-07-14%20at%208.58.15%E2%80%AFAM.png]
This is Josh Weil, U.S. Senate candidate from Florida, and let’s be clear, that’s not a political question. That’s a moral one.
And the reason this headline exists at all is because power-grab politicians like Ashley Moody handed Donald Trump and the MAGA movement the power to act without consequence .
They enabled him. Empowered him. Defended him through every scandal, every abuse of office, every disgraceful cover-up, and now with the Epstein memo fallout rocking Washington, the same Republicans are scrambling to decide if they still stand with the man they helped make untouchable.
But the damage is already done.
Here in Florida, we’ve seen how that kind of blind loyalty works. People like Ashley Moody will shout about “law and order” while bending the rules for the politically powerful. They’ll say they care about children and then stay silent when survivors need them most.
That’s complicity , and it is insulting to the people of Florida who already break their backs working in a crumbling economy because of these politicians.
I'm running for U.S. Senate to bring accountability back to our government. Because we deserve better than politicians who only speak up when it's convenient. If you’re with me, chip in right now. Let’s replace MAGA enablers with real public servants come 2026:
👉DONATE HERE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/coreemail_jw_kl]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
Express Donate: $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/coreemail_jw_kl?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=express_lane_10]
Express Donate: $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/coreemail_jw_kl?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=express_lane_25]
Express Donate: $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/coreemail_jw_kl?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=express_lane_50]
Express Donate: $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/coreemail_jw_kl?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=express_lane_100]
Or, donate another amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/coreemail_jw_kl?express_lane_other=true&refcode=express_lane_other]
We can’t undo the past but we can stop them from repeating it. Our teams are working hard on the ground to get our message out, but can only knock on as many doors as we have the funds to get them to.
Thank you,
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/387153444E713877456653376A556574376F314841345859327A4D665362374C42586753794B64635252546F6551413647436E7356536243345157355952664A/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-06-18%20at%2011.05.04%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Paid for by Josh Weil, Democrat for US Senate
Josh Weil for US Senate
5800 Beach Blvd. STE 203-337
Jacksonville, FL 32207
United States

| | The Golden Gate Bridge is a suspension bridge spanning the Golden Gate, the one-mile-wide (1.6 km) strait connecting San Francisco Bay and the Pacific Ocean in California, United States. The structure links San Francisco—the northern tip of the San Francisco Peninsula—to Marin County, carrying both U.S. Route 101 and California State Route 1 across the strait. It also carries pedestrian and bicycle traffic, and is designated as part of U.S. Bicycle Route 95. Recognized by the American Society of Civil Engineers as one of the Wonders of the Modern World,[7] the bridge is one of the most internationally recognized symbols of San Francisco and California. The idea of a fixed link between San Francisco and Marin had gained increasing popularity during the late 19th century, but it was not until the early 20th century that such a link became feasible. Joseph Strauss served as chief engineer for the project, with Leon Moisseiff, Irving Morrow and Charles Ellis making significant contributions to its design. The bridge opened to the public on May 27, 1937,[8] and has undergone various retrofits and other improvement projects in the decades since. The Golden Gate Bridge is described in Frommer's travel guide as "possibly the most beautiful, certainly the most photographed, bridge in the world."[9][10] At the time of its opening in 1937, it was both the longest and the tallest suspension bridge in the world, titles it held until 1964 and 1998 respectively. Its main span is 4,200 feet (1,280 m) and its total height is 746 feet (227 m).[11] History Ferry service Further information: Ferries of San Francisco Bay Before the bridge was built, the only practical short route between San Francisco and what is now Marin County was by boat across a section of San Francisco Bay. A ferry service began as early as 1820, with a regularly scheduled service beginning in the 1840s for the purpose of transporting water to San Francisco.[12] In 1867, the Sausalito Land and Ferry Company opened. In 1920, the service was taken over by the Golden Gate Ferry Company, which merged in 1929 with the ferry system of the Southern Pacific Railroad, becoming the Southern Pacific-Golden Gate Ferries, Ltd., the largest ferry operation in the world.[12][13] Once for railroad passengers and customers only, Southern Pacific's automobile ferries became very profitable and important to the regional economy.[14] The ferry crossing between the Hyde Street Pier in San Francisco and Sausalito Ferry Terminal in Marin County took approximately 20 minutes and cost $1.00 per vehicle prior to 1937, when the price was reduced to compete with the new bridge.[15][16] The trip from the San Francisco Ferry Building took 27 minutes. Many wanted to build a bridge to connect San Francisco to Marin County. San Francisco was the largest American city still served primarily by ferry boats. Because it did not have a permanent link with communities around the bay, the city's growth rate was below the national average.[17] Many experts said that a bridge could not be built across the 6,700-foot (2,000-metre) strait, which had strong, swirling tides and currents, with water 372 ft (113 m) deep[18] at the center of the channel, and frequent strong winds. Experts said that ferocious winds and blinding fogs would prevent construction and operation.[17] Conception Golden Gate with Fort Point in foreground, c. 1891 Although the idea of a bridge spanning the Golden Gate was not new, the proposal that eventually took hold was made in a 1916 San Francisco Bulletin article by former engineering student James Wilkins.[19] San Francisco's City Engineer estimated the cost at $100 million (equivalent to $2.8 billion in 2023), and impractical for the time. He asked bridge engineers whether it could be built for less.[12] One who responded, Joseph Strauss, was an ambitious engineer and poet who had, for his graduate thesis, designed a 55-mile-long (89 km) railroad bridge across the Bering Strait.[20] At the time, Strauss had completed some 400 drawbridges—most of which were inland—and nothing on the scale of the new project.[3] Strauss's initial drawings[21] were for a massive cantilever on each side of the strait, connected by a central suspension segment, which Strauss promised could be built for $17 million (equivalent to $476 million in 2023).[12] A suspension-bridge design was chosen, using recent advances in bridge design and metallurgy.[12] Strauss spent more than a decade drumming up support in Northern California.[22] The bridge faced opposition, including litigation, from many sources. The Department of War was concerned that the bridge would interfere with ship traffic. The US Navy feared that a ship collision or sabotage to the bridge could block the entrance to one of its main harbors. Unions demanded guarantees that local workers would be favored for construction jobs. Southern Pacific Railroad, one of the most powerful business interests in California, opposed the bridge as competition to its ferry fleet and filed a lawsuit against the project, leading to a mass boycott of the ferry service.[12] In May 1924, Colonel Herbert Deakyne held the second hearing on the Bridge on behalf of the Secretary of War in a request to use federal land for construction. Deakyne, on behalf of the Secretary of War, approved the transfer of land needed for the bridge structure and leading roads to the "Bridging the Golden Gate Association" and both San Francisco County and Marin County, pending further bridge plans by Strauss.[23] Another ally was the fledgling automobile industry, which supported the development of roads and bridges to increase demand for automobiles.[15] The bridge's name was first used when the project was initially discussed in 1917 by M.M. O'Shaughnessy, city engineer of San Francisco, and Strauss. The name became official with the passage of the Golden Gate Bridge and Highway District Act by the state legislature in 1923, creating a special district to design, build and finance the bridge.[24] San Francisco and most of the counties along the North Coast of California joined the Golden Gate Bridge District, with the exception being Humboldt County, whose residents opposed the bridge's construction and the traffic it would generate.[25] Design South tower seen from walkway, with Art Deco elements Strauss was the chief engineer in charge of the overall design and construction of the bridge project.[17] However, because he had little understanding or experience with cable-suspension designs,[26] responsibility for much of the engineering and architecture fell on other experts. Strauss's initial design proposal (two double cantilever spans linked by a central suspension segment) was unacceptable from a visual standpoint.[21] The final suspension design was conceived and championed by Leon Moisseiff, the engineer of the Manhattan Bridge in New York City.[27] Irving Morrow, a relatively unknown residential architect, designed the overall shape of the bridge towers, the lighting scheme, and Art Deco elements, such as the tower decorations, streetlights, railing, and walkways. The famous International Orange color was Morrow's personal selection, winning out over other possibilities, including the US Navy's suggestion that it be painted with black and yellow stripes to ensure visibility by passing ships.[17][28] Senior engineer Charles Alton Ellis, collaborating remotely with Moisseiff, was the principal engineer of the project.[29] Moisseiff produced the basic structural design, introducing his "deflection theory" by which a thin, flexible roadway would flex in the wind, greatly reducing stress by transmitting forces via suspension cables to the bridge towers.[29] Although the Golden Gate Bridge design has proved sound, a later Moisseiff design, the original Tacoma Narrows Bridge, collapsed in a strong windstorm soon after it was completed, because of an unexpected aeroelastic flutter.[30] Ellis was also tasked with designing a "bridge within a bridge" in the southern abutment, to avoid the need to demolish Fort Point, a pre–Civil War masonry fortification viewed, even then, as worthy of historic preservation. He penned a graceful steel arch spanning the fort and carrying the roadway to the bridge's southern anchorage.[31] Below Golden Gate Bridge Ellis was a Greek scholar and mathematician who at one time was a University of Illinois professor of engineering despite having no engineering degree. He eventually earned a degree in civil engineering from the University of Illinois prior to designing the Golden Gate Bridge and spent the last twelve years of his career as a professor at Purdue University. He became an expert in structural design, writing the standard textbook of the time.[32] Ellis did much of the technical and theoretical work that built the bridge, but he received none of the credit in his lifetime. In November 1931, Strauss fired Ellis and replaced him with a former subordinate, Clifford Paine, ostensibly for wasting too much money sending telegrams back and forth to Moisseiff.[32] Ellis, obsessed with the project and unable to find work elsewhere during the Depression, continued working 70 hours per week on an unpaid basis, eventually turning in ten volumes of hand calculations.[32] With an eye toward self-promotion and posterity, Strauss downplayed the contributions of his collaborators who, despite receiving little recognition or compensation,[26] are largely responsible for the final form of the bridge. He succeeded in having himself credited as the person most responsible for the design and vision of the bridge.[32] Only much later were the contributions of the others on the design team properly appreciated.[32] In May 2007, the Golden Gate Bridge District issued a formal report on 70 years of stewardship of the famous bridge and decided to give Ellis major credit for the design of the bridge. Panorama showing the height, depth, and length of the span from end to end, looking west Panorama of the Golden Gate Bridge at sunset, as seen from just north of Alcatraz Island Finance The Golden Gate Bridge and Highway District, authorized by an act of the California Legislature, was incorporated in 1928 as the official entity to design, construct, and finance the Golden Gate Bridge.[17] However, after the Wall Street Crash of 1929, the District was unable to raise the construction funds, so it lobbied for a $30 million bond measure (equivalent to $532 million today). The bonds were approved in November 1930,[20] by votes in the counties affected by the bridge.[33] The construction budget at the time of approval was $27 million ($492 million today). However, the District was unable to sell the bonds until 1932, when Amadeo Giannini, the founder of San Francisco–based Bank of America, agreed on behalf of his bank to buy the entire issue in order to help the local economy.[12] Construction Construction began on January 5, 1933.[12] The project cost more than $35 million[34] ($610 million in 2023 dollars[35]), and was completed ahead of schedule and $1.3 million under budget (equivalent to $28.9 million today).[36] The Golden Gate Bridge construction project was carried out by the McClintic-Marshall Construction Co., a subsidiary of Bethlehem Steel Corporation founded by Howard H. McClintic and Charles D. Marshall, both of Lehigh University. An original rivet replaced during the seismic retrofit after the 1989 Loma Prieta earthquake. A total of 1.2 million steel rivets hold the bridge's two towers together. Strauss remained head of the project, overseeing day-to-day construction and making some groundbreaking contributions. A graduate of the University of Cincinnati, he placed a brick from his alma mater's demolished McMicken Hall in the south anchorage before the concrete was poured. Strauss also innovated the use of movable safety netting beneath the men working, which saved many lives. Nineteen men saved by the nets over the course of the project formed the Half Way to Hell Club. Nonetheless, eleven men were killed in falls, ten on February 17, 1937, when a scaffold (secured by undersized bolts) with twelve men on it fell into and broke through the safety net; two of the twelve survived the 200-foot (61 m) fall into the water.[37][38] The Round House Café diner was then included in the southeastern end of the Golden Gate Bridge, adjacent to the tourist plaza which was renovated in 2012.[39] The Round House Café, an Art Deco design by Alfred Finnila completed in 1938, has been popular throughout the years as a starting point for various commercial tours of the bridge and an unofficial gift shop.[40] The diner was renovated in 2012[39] and the gift shop was then removed as a new, official gift shop has been included in the adjacent plaza.[40] During the bridge work, the Assistant Civil Engineer of California Alfred Finnila had overseen the entire iron work of the bridge as well as half of the bridge's road work.[41] Contributors Plaque of the major contributors to the Golden Gate Bridge lists contractors, engineering-staff, directors and officers:[42] Contractors Foundations - Pacific Bridge Company Anchorages - Barrett & Hilp Structural steel - Main span - Bethlehem Steel Company Incorporated Approach steel - J.H.
