| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3119ca3f-1a2f-4bc5-bf04-a7a60827ac18?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
You've probably heard about that Texas Libertarian group wreaking havoc in Idaho, stoking division, strife, and pure chaos.
Now they've gone all in with a bizarre, smear-filled hit piece, riddled with half-truths, shamelessly targeting Idaho GOP Chair Dorothy Moon.
I was ready to lay out a post exposing their game, but it turns out, someone else already connected the dots:
> » **[YAL's War on Idaho Conservatives](https://substack.com/redirect/43ca6ecd-3574-40dc-871b-0045ed791658?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
Everyone in Idaho needs to read this and distance themselves from this out-of-state organization sowing strife and chaos in Idaho.

It's sad to me because they used to run a tight ship a long time ago.
_**But their actions turned so vile that I had to cut ties completely in 2024.**_
Because I couldn't stomach their tactics, and I couldn't sleep at night knowing they were manipulating legislators to sow chaos and stir up trouble. Worst of all, they masquerade as conservatives while unapologetically presenting as hardline Libertarians at their conferences.
You can [read my short post on THAT here](https://substack.com/redirect/f9de000b-9dd2-4da6-bd52-3b4d038ff03f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) if you'd like.

subject: Justice Sotomayor just called this decision“indefensible.”
disclaimer_text: paid for by DLCC Victory Fund, 1225 Eye Street NW Suite 1250, Washington, DC 20005
body:
Protecting students, parents, and public schools from funding cuts through state legislative action has never been more important. | | |
---
| |
---|---

name: Team Wes Moore
subject: First line of defense
body:
[ https://act.wesmoore.com/go/9?t=1001&akid=307%2E1086043%2E469SNT ]Wes Moore for Governor[ https://act.wesmoore.com/go/9?t=1002&akid=307%2E1086043%2E469SNT ]Wes Moore for Governor

💪🦀 Wes is fighting for Maryland so that all our families can thrive. But
we have to be honest: It’s an uphill battle.

The Trump administration’s disastrous budget bill is set to rip Medicaid
and SNAP away from thousands of Marylanders. We’re talking about veterans,
seniors, and working families.

Not only that: Maryland is not a blue state. Two of the last four
governors of Maryland have been Republicans. Just three years ago we had a
Republican governor.

Wes has his work cut out for him, whether it is fighting for
Marylanders or winning
this race. So he’s counting on your support. When it matters most, will
you chip in to make sure we Leave No One Behind?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250714-1?refcode=em-20250714-1&refcode2=307_1086043_469SNT&akid=307%2E1086043%2E469SNT ]Our campaign depends on grassroots support from folks like you, and our
average donation this quarter is just about $24.77. Will you chip in any
amount today?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250714-1?amount=5&refcode=em-20250714-1&refcode2=307_1086043_469SNT&akid=307%2E1086043%2E469SNT ]Donate $5 ››

We’re glad you’re with us in this movement,
Peter.

— Team Wes Moore

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.wesmoore.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=307%2E1086043%2E469SNT

This leaves voters vulnerable to what Republican state legislators have been pushing across the South: voter purges, barriers to casting a ballot, discriminatory redistricting, and various other forms of voter suppression. **Make no mistake, this is intentional** , and is a part of a broader effort by far-right extremists and the Trump Regime to retain power, restrict voting freedom, and further silence historically disenfranchised groups. **Fewer civil rights protections equate to more voter suppression, especially for Black and brown communities, as well as young and first-time voters.[We can’t let this go without a fight. Lack of enforcement of civil rights protections isn’t the only thing that’s leaving voters vulnerable.
The Trump Regime has fully dropped critical lawsuits, including a challenge against a Georgia voter suppression law, SB 202. In 2021, Brian Kemp signed SB 202 into law, which includes various anti-voter measures:
* Challenges to early voting rules
* Strict voter ID requirements
* Restricting who can assist voters who need help filling out their absentee ballots
* Limiting drop boxes in every county
* Barring people from handing out water and food for those waiting in line to vote
* Restricting vote-by-mail

**A whopping six lawsuits have been filed against SB 202** , including the Department of Justice’s (DOJ's) original lawsuit from 2021. These cases, now consolidated and continuing without the DOJ, argue that the law violates the Voting Rights Act, the Civil Rights Act, and the Constitution. Before Trump, the DOJ was fighting to defend Black voters, arguing that the bill intended to “deny or abridge the right of Black Georgians to vote on account of race or color.”
**Far-right extremists are using the same old playbook: Cling to power by restricting voting freedom, violating civil rights, and silencing the people. **

| **ABOUT MARTY**
**Marty O’Donnell isn’t a politician** and he’s not taking any corporate PAC money. Marty will give us a voice – not special interests or Wall Street. It’s time to turn our nation around to make life better for Nevadans.

**Marty O’Donnell has an ear for solving problems.** He’s an award-winning composer who wrote the iconic soundtracks for the video games Halo and Destiny. Marty will cut through the noise in DC.

**Marty O’Donnell will be a champion for Nevada families.** As a husband, father, and grandfather, Marty can’t sit on the sidelines as local families struggle because of the problems created by Big Government.

name: Costello for U.S. Senate
subject: Let's Work to Fix What’s Broken - Campaign Update
body:
Like hundreds of other dedicated Americans, I was proud to work for USAID in the 1990s and early 2000s.

| | | | | [|
---](https://costelloforsenate.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d37adbc910a9a40e00b14f5&id=93732cf930&e=5d7176e969)
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://costelloforsenate.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d37adbc910a9a40e00b14f5&id=c3575e9354&e=5d7176e969)
| Friends, FYI below is a copy of David’s recent Bangor Daily News Op Ed on the harmful dismantling of USAID.
**USAID, A Detrimental and Unwarranted Demise** The United States Agency for International Development (USAID) ceased to exist on July 1, 2025, and its reckless and senseless demise will likely haunt American foreign policy and global interests for years to come. Created by President John F. Kennedy, USAID was an agency that for more than 60 years bolstered America’s reputation, influence, and interests abroad by providing needed humanitarian, economic, and political development assistance. Like hundreds of other dedicated Americans, I was proud to work for USAID in the 1990s and early 2000s. My work centered on promoting peace, democracy, and economic stability in such conflict-ravaged countries as Cambodia, Haiti, Bosnia, and Kosovo. In Cambodia, we worked alongside the United Nations to ensure Cambodia’s first free and fair election after years of war and unspeakable genocide. In Haiti, we worked closely with the U.S. Army to implement more than 2,000 community improvement projects, from the drilling of wells to the construction of schools and clinics. In the Balkans, I established the first U.S. government office in the then sharply anti-American Republika Srpska Bosnia and ultimately worked regionally with U.S. and NATO militaries, and European diplomats and non-governmental organizations (NGOs), to implement more than 2,300 peace-building projects. Projects that, working with courageous local partners, undermined hateful nationalist propaganda, organizations, and objectives. In all instances, our work and the work of others at USAID stabilized communities and countries, saved countless lives and mitigated the migration of thousands of refugees. And although, as is the case with all government agencies, there was room for improvement at USAID, the agency and the vast majority of its vital programs did not deserve to be dismantled. A recent analysis of USAID by the distinguished medical journal, _The Lancet_ , concluded that from 2001 through 2021, programs funded by USAID prevented nearly 92 million deaths in 133 countries. In addition, it estimates that the cuts the Trump administration has made to USAID will result in more than 14 million deaths in the next five years. About 4.5 million will be children under 5.Alarmingly, the Trump Administration’s current approach of gutting foreign aid, cozying up to dictators, disrespecting allies, and withdrawing from critical environmental, medical, and humanitarian agreements is sowing chaos and weakening America’s standing around the world. I’m running for the United States Senate today, in part, to help resurrect the critical work of USAID and other essential agencies and improve the standing and performance of our federal government, both at home in the U.S. and abroad. America is a great nation, yet it’s been at its best when it’s been fully, intelligently, and benevolently engaged in world affairs. It’s time to alter course and for America to behave at its best again! David Costello, Democratic Candidate for the U.S. Senate from Maine in 2026
Again, thank you for supporting our efforts to **unseat Senator Susan Collins** and end the Trump Administration and GOP Congress’s reckless assault on our democracy, environment, and economy. Winning over the U.S. Senate in 2026 is key to ending the turmoil in Washington, and ensuring that all Americans, not just the wealthy, are provided with the education, job skills, and other life enriching opportunities to thrive. Senator Collins has done little to prevent the Trump Administration and her Republican colleagues from undertaking a range of immensely harmful actions, from the curtailment of vital medical research to the elimination of essential government agencies, like USAID. If you, like me, believe it’s time to retire Senator Collins and win back the U.S. Senate, **please share this email with others and consider contributing $20** , or any amount that works for you, to aid in our efforts to defeat Senator Collins and begin the hard work of fixing our imperfect and terribly broken federal government. Thank you and best always,David

One year after that fateful day in Butler, PA, do we have more questions than answers? It's been a year since the assassination attempt on President Trump's life, it was the most-defining moment in the campaign as a defiant, heroic President Trump stood up after being grazed by a bullet and said "Fight, Fight, Fight." Salena Zito was about 6 feet away from the president on that day and brings us insight to what happened and the aftermath. President Trump called Salena 7 times that day, something had changed, he was saved by God and the profundity was just starting to crystalize. Salena shares intimate details about Butler in her best-selling book Butler: The Untold Story of the Near Assassination of Donald Trump and the Fight for America's Heartland. We still don't know what radicalized Thomas Crooks and his family is a mystery in their neighborhood. We remember Corey Comperatore, his family and all those that suffered in Butler. Susan Crabtree has been investigating the Secret Service since they completely failed to secure the perimeter. Former Secret Service director Kim Cheatle is largely to blame as reports came out weeks in advance about an attempt on President Trump's life. Another part of the puzzle is Iran, Asif Merchant was casing the Secret Service for months trying to find a way to infiltrate their defenses. Iran has also released mock videos of a drone attack on President Trump. Senator Rand Paul and Senator Chuck Grassley, have produced daming reports on the extent of the failure and Susan wants to know if the new director of the Secret Service Sean Curran is explicitly addressing these former failures of the agency.
Featuring:
**Salena Zito**
Journalist & Author
https://salenazito.com/
Get you copy of Salena's best-selling book here: https://a.co/d/autJKD1

name: Team Bennet
subject: A record of fighting for our state.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q3?refcode=X_250714_publiclandsrecord_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q3?refcode=X_250714_publiclandsrecord_act]
Hi team,
If there is one thing Coloradans hold close to our hearts, it’s our public lands. Our lands have incredible cultural, economic, and recreational value that people across the world travel to experience for themselves.
Michael Bennet knows this and, for years, has been a leading advocate for protecting and preserving our public lands:
🌎 Most recently, Michael helped kill Senator Mike Lee's provision in Trump’s horrendous bill that would’ve allowed 3.3 million acres of public lands, including in Colorado, to be sold to fossil fuel companies and real estate developers.
🌎 From Chimney Rock, to Brown’s Canyon, to Camp Amache, Michael's helped protect hundreds of thousands of acres of precious lands, including over 53,000 acres within the Camp Hale-Continental Divide National Monument.
🌎 Alongside Senator John Hickenlooper, Michael reintroduced the Dolores River National Conservation Area and Special Management Area Act, which would protect an additional 68,000 acres in Southwestern Colorado. They also teamed up to write the GORP Act to protect over 730,000 acres in and around the Gunnison River Basin.
Michael has a record of fighting for our state and succeeding—a record he’s ready to bring to the Governor’s office.
As Governor, Michael will build on Colorado’s legacy and leadership by expanding our network of parks, making strategic investments in conservation, and setting a model for the country's recreational infrastructure.
Michael has been and will continue to be a leader for our public lands. If you’re ready to support his campaign, please chip in today! Even a few bucks make a difference. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q3?refcode=X_250714_publiclandsrecord_act]

clean_body: [] Hi team, If there is one thing Coloradans hold close to our hearts, it’s our public lands. Our lands have incredible cultural, economic, and recreational value that people across the world travel to experience for themselves. Michael Bennet knows this and, for years, has been a leading advocate for protecting and preserving our public lands: 🌎 Most recently, Michael helped kill Senator Mike Lee's provision in Trump’s horrendous bill that would’ve allowed 3.3 million acres of public lands, including in Colorado, to be sold to fossil fuel companies and real estate developers. 🌎 From Chimney Rock, to Brown’s Canyon, to Camp Amache, Michael's helped protect hundreds of thousands of acres of precious lands, including over 53,000 acres within the Camp Hale-Continental Divide National Monument. 🌎 Alongside Senator John Hickenlooper, Michael reintroduced the Dolores River National Conservation Area and Special Management Area Act, which would protect an additional 68,000 acres in Southwestern Colorado. They also teamed up to write the GORP Act to protect over 730,000 acres in and around the Gunnison River Basin. Michael has a record of fighting for our state and succeeding—a record he’s ready to bring to the Governor’s office. As Governor, Michael will build on Colorado’s legacy and leadership by expanding our network of parks, making strategic investments in conservation, and setting a model for the country's recreational infrastructure. Michael has been and will continue to be a leader for our public lands. If you’re ready to support his campaign, please chip in today! Even a few bucks make a difference. [] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: DONATE $5 [] DONATE $10 [] DONATE $25 [] DONATE $50 [] DONATE $100 [] OTHER [] Thank you, Team Bennet [] [] [] Bennet for Governor PO Box 270117 Louisville, CO 80027 United States WWW.MICHAELBENNET.COM [] | INFO@MICHAELBENNET.COM [info@michaelbennet.com] | PRIVACY POLICY [] Paid for by Bennet for Governor. name: Tammy Baldwin
subject: I know what it’s like
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
| I had a "pre-existing condition.”
# [](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1&akid=4871%2E35263%2ESDQvq2)
Peter, I know what it's like to worry about whether you can afford the care you need. Growing up, I faced the challenges of having a "pre-existing condition" that left my grandparents struggling to find affordable insurance. No family should have to worry about getting the health care they need. Yet, for millions of Americans, including many right here in Wisconsin, that fear is becoming a harsh reality. Trump's budget bill threatens to slash Medicaid funding, putting the health care of countless Wisconsinites on the chopping block. This is not just a budget issue — it's a moral one, and we must fight back. Health care is a fundamental right, not a privilege. That's why I've dedicated my career to expanding access to affordable healthcare, fighting for protections for people with pre-existing conditions, and working to lower prescription drug prices so families don't have to choose between medicine and other basic needs. Each of us deserves the peace of mind that comes from knowing we can access the care we need, when we need it. I'm committed to standing up against these devastating cuts and ensuring every American has access to quality care. **[As we push forward in this fight, we're also facing a crucial mid-month fundraising goal, and meeting it is vital to continue battling these harmful policies. Can I count on your help to defend health care access?](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/4438?c0=stb-em-july25.PrghHS&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_MMG_AC_20250714_PM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=4871%2E35263%2ESDQvq2)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/3664?c0=stb-em-july25.yzPNVJ&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_MMG_AC_20250714_PM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=4871%2E35263%2ESDQvq2)

clean_body: | I had a "pre-existing condition.” Peter, I know what it's like to worry about whether you can afford the care you need. Growing up, I faced the challenges of having a "pre-existing condition" that left my grandparents struggling to find affordable insurance. No family should have to worry about getting the health care they need. Yet, for millions of Americans, including many right here in Wisconsin, that fear is becoming a harsh reality. Trump's budget bill threatens to slash Medicaid funding, putting the health care of countless Wisconsinites on the chopping block. This is not just a budget issue — it's a moral one, and we must fight back. Health care is a fundamental right, not a privilege. That's why I've dedicated my career to expanding access to affordable healthcare, fighting for protections for people with pre-existing conditions, and working to lower prescription drug prices so families don't have to choose between medicine and other basic needs. Each of us deserves the peace of mind that comes from knowing we can access the care we need, when we need it. I'm committed to standing up against these devastating cuts and ensuring every American has access to quality care. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | Together, we can build a future where every Wisconsinite has the opportunity to live a healthy, and secure life. name: Team Gillibrand
subject: Censorship. Book bans. We can’t sit this out.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
Kirsten needs our help to protect public schools and elect Democrats who will fight for them.
Back-to-school season is supposed to mean new backpacks, fresh notebooks and a clean slate. Unfortunately, thanks to Trump and his allies, it’s also come to mean something else entirely. Book bans. Censorship in classrooms. Political attacks on teachers and curriculum. Instead of supporting public schools, Trump’s extremist agenda is targeting them — and putting our kids’ education and safety on the line. That’s why Kirsten is working hard — not just in the Senate, but as Chair of the DSCC — to elect Democrats who will protect inclusive, high-quality public education for every child. [**_Will you chip in $5 or more to help us elect strong Democrats and stop Trump’s education attacks in their tracks?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111472396/557375059/246949951?amount=5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.07.14_Mid-Month-BTS_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZDg4YTk5Zi1mZTYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oZ8DMA_zyAJ-rnS4BDQOI7BGU5ilj1JNNa65dKV5NsM=) | | [DONATE >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111472397/557375060/246949951?amount=5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.07.14_Mid-Month-BTS_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZDg4YTk5Zi1mZTYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oZ8DMA_zyAJ-rnS4BDQOI7BGU5ilj1JNNa65dKV5NsM=)

We cannot let extremists decide what our kids learn or whose stories deserve to be told. We need leaders who trust teachers, respect students and show up for working families — not special interests or political agendas. And we need them in Congress now.
Your support will help us build the grassroots momentum we need to win this fight. Every contribution goes directly toward helping Democrats organize, get on the air and meet voters where they are.
[**_Chip in today to help us elect more Democrats who will protect public education, defend our teachers and fight for the truth in our classrooms._**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111472398/557375061/246949951?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.07.14_Mid-Month-BTS_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZDg4YTk5Zi1mZTYwLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oZ8DMA_zyAJ-rnS4BDQOI7BGU5ilj1JNNa65dKV5NsM=)
Thank you for being in this with us.
Team Gillibrand

name: Justice Democrats
subject: He should be censured for his racist, Islamophobic attacks
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Right-wing Rep. Randy Fine is once again launching racist, Islamophobic
attacks at Justice Democrat Rep. Ilhan Omar.

Last May, Fine got away with the baseless claim that Ilhan “endorses and
supports Muslim terror” just because she’s a Muslim American who opposes
war crimes against Palestinians. Now, he’s doubled down on these
disgusting smears:

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/em20250714-signup-censurerandyfine?source=em20250714-36842&akid=36842%2E1203867%2Ek2pj5c ]Screenshot of a tweet from Congressman Randy Fine replying to Rep.
Ilhan Omar. The tweet reads: 'I'm sure it is difficult to see us welcome
the killer of so many of your fellow Muslim terrorists. The only shame is
that you serve in Congress.' Below is a quoted tweet from Ilhan Omar that
reads: 'This is Benjamin Netanyahu’s third visit to DC this year. War
criminals should not be welcomed by any president or Congress....'

As Ilhan’s fellow Squad member Rep. Ayanna Pressley stated, this is
dangerous, Islamophobic rhetoric aimed at a Congresswoman for cheap
political points.

This kind of language threatens the safety of Ilhan and other Muslims
across our country, including Fine’s own constituents, and it must not be
tolerated.

Fine shouldn’t have the honor of serving in Congress, but we know he won’t
be forced out. At the very least, he should be censured by his colleagues
for the latest in his pattern of vile, racist attacks.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/em20250714-signup-censurerandyfine?source=em20250714-36842&akid=36842%2E1203867%2Ek2pj5c ]Add your name right now to demand that Congress formally vote to
censure MAGA clout-chaser Rep. Randy Fine for his disgusting attacks on
Ilhan!

clean_body: [1]Justice Democrats Peter, Right-wing Rep. Randy Fine is once again launching racist, Islamophobic attacks at Justice Democrat Rep. Ilhan Omar. Last May, Fine got away with the baseless claim that Ilhan “endorses and supports Muslim terror” just because she’s a Muslim American who opposes war crimes against Palestinians. Now, he’s doubled down on these disgusting smears: [ ]Screenshot of a tweet from Congressman Randy Fine replying to Rep. Ilhan Omar. The tweet reads: 'I'm sure it is difficult to see us welcome the killer of so many of your fellow Muslim terrorists. The only shame is that you serve in Congress.' Below is a quoted tweet from Ilhan Omar that reads: 'This is Benjamin Netanyahu’s third visit to DC this year. War criminals should not be welcomed by any president or Congress....' As Ilhan’s fellow Squad member Rep. Ayanna Pressley stated, this is dangerous, Islamophobic rhetoric aimed at a Congresswoman for cheap political points. This kind of language threatens the safety of Ilhan and other Muslims across our country, including Fine’s own constituents, and it must not be tolerated. Fine shouldn’t have the honor of serving in Congress, but we know he won’t be forced out. At the very least, he should be censured by his colleagues for the latest in his pattern of vile, racist attacks. [ ]Add your name right now to demand that Congress formally vote to censure MAGA clout-chaser Rep. Randy Fine for his disgusting attacks on Ilhan! [ ]Add Your Name It’s time for this bigot to finally be held accountable. In solidarity, Justice Democrats Contribute to Justice Democrats:

name: Justin Kwasa, LCV
subject: Good Trouble Lives On National Day of Action is Thursday -- RSVP today
body:
Join us for a day of action on July 17th

If you are unable to see the message below, [click here to view](https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTI2OTk1MDkS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjI2MDE2MDgyNAS2&mt=1&rt=0).
| | | |
---
|
* * *
---
| | | | Peter,
---
| | Good Trouble Lives On is a national day of action on **Thursday, July 17th** to respond to the attacks on our civil and human rights by the Trump administration. LCV is proud to partner with hundreds of organizations across the country to build on the resistance movement that the Hands Off and No Kings protests started earlier this year. Together, we'll mobilize millions of people to show Trump that in the United States, the power lies with the people. Coined by the late civil rights leader Congressman John Lewis, "Good Trouble" is the action of coming together to take peaceful, nonviolent action to challenge injustice and create meaningful change.
---
| | [**_RSVP today and click here to find the Good Trouble Lives On event closest to you. _**](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/gryxf4a-K2TR/TEYxZ1ZLWkhac0wwMEVsK3ZlUWl3Mkp4TldFdGRLdVhIQnZKZ3NpS1dLaW02OC8wME9xTkR3V3praHA5YVQ5SFJtODRyYUtPbVlvQW51dk9zWk1YVFdkQlIweFE0QlhWSHhucnJJKytYM0E9S0/)
---
| | The civil rights leaders of the past have shown us the power of collective action. That’s why on **July 17th** , five years after the passing of Congressman John Lewis, communities across the country will take to the streets, courthouses, and community spaces to carry forward his fight for justice, voting rights, and dignity for all. With over 1200 events taking place across the country, it is easy for you to join the movement. From voter suppression bills like the SAVE Act to the criminalization of protest, the Trump administration is carrying out a full-scale attack on our civil and human rights. But we know the truth: in this country, the power lies with the people, and we’re rising to prove it.
---
| | [**_Roin us for a national day of nonviolent action as we rally, march, host teach-ins, press conferences, and vigils to demand that Congress fight back and protect our freedom to vote. This is a moment for moral clarity and collective power._**](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/EZ_RcA8vrEQE/TEYxZ1ZLWkhac0wwMEVsK3ZlUWl3Mkp4TldFdGRLdVhIQnZKZ3NpS1dLaW02OC8wME9xTkR3V3praHA5YVQ5SFJtODRyYUtPbVlvQW51dk9zWk1YVFdkQlIweFE0QlhWSHhucnJJKytYM0E9S0/)
---
| | Thank you for being with us Peter. We march in peace. We act in power. Good Trouble Lives On,
---
| | Justin Kwasa
Democracy Program Director
League of Conservation Voters
---
| | |
* * *
---
| | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you,[ _update your contact information_](https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/ContactInformation?spMailingID=12699509&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjI2MDE2MDgyNAS2&spReportId=MjI2MDE2MDgyNAS2) _._ [_Click here to unsubscribe_](https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/Unsubscribe?spMailingID=12699509&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjI2MDE2MDgyNAS2&spReportId=MjI2MDE2MDgyNAS2) from our supporter list. [_Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback here_](mailto:feedback@lcv.org?subject=) , or just reply to this email! Thanks for your support.
**740 15th St NW, Suite 700**
**Washington, DC 20005**

**Beth Van Duyne**

**GET TO KNOW BETH:
** | Beth is a single, working mother of two amazing kids. Every day, she reminds them that we live in the greatest state, in the greatest nation, in the history of the world.

Where else but the United States could a girl leave home at the age of 17, put herself through college, become the first female Mayor of her hometown, and then be asked to serve America in a presidential administration?

**Beth Van Duyne is running for Congress because she knows the people of the 24th District deserve to have a strong, principled voice in Congress.**

She's been asked what prompted her to run for public office; the answer is simple – her children. We live in a very fragile time for our nation, our families, and the future of the American Dream. Socialism is on the rise, our border crisis has never been worse, and we face constant threats from hostile nations willing to use cyber-attacks, nuclear weapons, and terrorist jihad. When Beth thinks about her two children, she wants them to have the same opportunities in life that she did; She wants them to be able to grow up safe and proud of our country.

**Beth is a single mom, a conservative Republican, a reformer who has defeated government corruption, a believer in the rule of law** who has helped remove thousands of criminal aliens from North Texas, a leader who helped deliver growth and new job opportunities, and a Constitutionalist who has never backed down when it comes to protecting the rights of all North Texans.

Beth is a single mom, a conservative Republican, a reformer who has defeated government corruption, a believer in the rule of law who has helped remove thousands of criminal aliens from North Texas, a leader who helped deliver growth and new job opportunities, and a Constitutionalist who has never backed down when it comes to protecting the rights of all North Texans. | PAID FOR BY BETH VAN DUYNE FOR CONGRESS

name: Rudy Giuliani
subject: [OFFICIAL] THE PEOPLE‘S PARDON OF RUDY GIULIANI
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Legal Defense Trust
body:
Waiting for your signature.
Waiting for your signature.
[View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e19586783/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3Yvdk5kYnpCSHo2ag/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTA3MTQ/LbmNsRW04U2pSOA/qP2hpZHM9bmMzRVhJUWE4c2U1R2dscw/gaHWDUg/s6m7b24cb55)

| [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Dear Patriot,
---
The Radical Democrats think they’ve finally got me.
---
**You see, a Democrat Attorney General is making me stand in a rigged trial for bogus state-level crimes, because she knows that not even the most powerful man on the face of the planet – President Trump – can save me with a _federal_ pardon in _state_ court.**
---
_I’m left with no escape and no lifeline, especially after they’ve already STRIPPED me of my law licenses!_
---
But even though the most powerful man in the country legally cannot step in to save me from being **JAILED** for the rest of my life, the most powerful force absolutely can: _THE AMERICAN PEOPLE_.
---
With my back against the wall, I’m turning to the _ONE_ _THING_ that can save me: _**the collective voices of MILLIONS of patriots like YOU.**_
---
[**Please help me reach my life-saving goal (_truly!_) today by immediately adding your name to “THE PEOPLE’S PARDON OF RUDY GIULIANI” as citizens unite to call for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES against me.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
__
---

[ADD YOUR NAME](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---

I’ve taken on mobsters. I’ve faced terrorists. I’ve battled corruption. I’ve been through it all. _Or so I thought..._
---
Never did I imagine that I would be the centerpiece of one of the Democrats’ most vindictive plots in history.
---
This entire plan by the Deep State to destroy a close friend of the President’s is truly the type of sinister scheme you’d think would only exist in the movies.
---
_But believe me, it exists; I’m living it! And if I fail to win this trial and defeat these sham charges, the Democrats will be able to jail me for the rest of my years on God’s green Earth._
---
These Deep State thugs know I have no pardon coming. No protection. No cavalry coming over the hill...
---
_...But what they continue to underestimate time and again is the collective strength of millions of faithful patriots like you who also _REFUSE_ to surrender to the Left’s tyranny. _
---
They entered my mugshot under booking #2313757, so my goal is for 2,313,757 patriots to sign my official petition — [**“THE PEOPLE’S PARDON OF RUDY GIULIANI**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)” — calling for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES.
---
_At this final hour, can I count on you, Patriot?_
---
[**Please help me reach my life-saving goal (_truly!_) today by immediately adding your name to “THE PEOPLE’S PARDON OF RUDY GIULIANI” as citizens unite to call for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES against me.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---

[ADD YOUR NAME](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---

Thank you and God bless you,
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Rudy Giuliani
Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund
---

---

---

Our strategy to flip Virginia blue depends on the small donations of people like you.

We know you're receiving loads of emails and texts asking for money right now, but we kindly ask that you read this message from beginning to end.
---

name: Christina Bohannan (personal)
subject: A little more fuel in the tank
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
| Peter,

It’s hard to put into words how grateful I am for the support you’ve shown since we launched this campaign. In just one month, I’ve seen more energy, heart, and momentum than most campaigns see in a year — and it’s reminded me of exactly why I’m in this fight.

Iowa is where I chose to raise my daughter, and it’s where I’ve built a life surrounded by neighbors who are kind and believe in standing together when times get tough. This place and its people mean the world to me, and I know how much better we could all be with leadership that listens and puts us first.

In 2024, we came within just 799 votes of flipping this seat. That’s not a long shot. That’s one good day of organizing, a handful of conversations on front porches, and a little more fuel in the tank.

_We didn’t get that close by accident._ We did it because so many people came together and put in the work, believing that our voices could carry further than any attack ad or political spin. That work gave us a real shot, and now, it’s our job to pick it up and carry it all the way across the finish line in 2026.

This campaign isn’t backed by billionaires or big party machines. It’s powered by people like you. That’s why every single action we take makes a difference. If you’re able, I’d be honored if you’d chip in before midnight to help us close the gap on our mid-month fundraising goal and keep moving forward >> **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cb-q3-2025?refcode2=dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111472976/557377077/-1612730695?refcode=em25_250714_MM3_fnd_gq_act___CB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250714_MM3_fnd_gq_act___CB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=af90cd4a-0161-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZjkwY2Q0YS0wMTYxLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zlQWJ14E5EnG6xaaTndiVwb6y_baW03JkXH-_u1f8Kk=)**

Thank you for believing in this work, and in what we can build together.

— Christina |

### **The American Dream Is Now a European Vacation**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/62497877-d965-4e56-9cf3-7d1811d41fdb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**LaGuardia Airport, July 13th**
As I sit here waiting for my connection to Cincy, the one that was supposed to leave from JFK but..cancelled. Then it was to slingshot me outta this other outer-borough airport by 8pm et...but creeps later and later into the evening with delays...
As this has me wondering when I'll get home, I'm also really having a hard time answering another question that's on repeat--like a broken record or treasonous Trump acts--in my head. Why did I come back?
Yeah, I know the obvious reasons. It's where my wife and I are raising our kids. We have older family members we wish to be here for. Etc. But the more existential one, I gotta tell you, I'm having some trouble with that.
For the first time in a while, I'm seriously thinking about a not-too-distant future when I don't call our reality-bites politics and eternally exhausting, dog-squatting-by-a-fire-hydrant culture, home.
Yes, I just flew in from 10 days in Rome and Sicily, and it's always hard to leave. Especially when it's these areas on the Mediterranean I've found myself returning to again and again (I lived in Nice, France, and have spent ~2 months of my life in and around Italy).
But it still feels different this time.
And it's not the pasta (ok, maybe a bit). Or the incredible wine (ok, maybe...). Or even the gorgeous Mediterranean sun and fantastically fun people I always meet.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9db8e5c4-c610-4177-97eb-cd493f0b8042?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
It's that these fantastically fun people live in a country where hav _ing a lif_ e is still a thing, and the government, both national and local, still function like they're supposed to: _to serve the people_. And this is Italy under Prime Minister Georgia Meloni, who runs one of the most conservative governments the country's had since World War II.
Let's call to two items above personal culture and political culture. They interact to a great degree, as, for example, the control you exercise over your body will certainly going to influence your ability to enjoy passions and hobbies. But some of this is just a general belief system regarding what government should do and work/life balance.
America is drastically failing on both of these, like Stephen Miller trying to keep his wife from pulling a "The Graduate" with Elon Musk.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/34866890-de24-49aa-b13f-6636b62dccaf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Regular people in Italy aren't stressed, anxious and depressed like we are--_especially our kids_ --for a variety of reasons. Nobody worries they won't be able to afford to treat an illness, because _it just never would happen_. Like every sane democracy, which is to say every single one but ours, healthcare is universal because _**OF COURSE IT IS**_.
Like knowing Trump would be in Jeffrey Epstein's little black book. Universal, unless you're a MAGA birdbrain with a chronically malfunctioning sympathetic nervous system, such that fight or flight is set off by schools without Christmas trees or Superman movies.
But back to our European friends, specifically in Rome and Sicily. They don't have to start saving for their children's college from their own first job post-puberty because it wouldn't cross their minds they'd fall hundreds of thousands of Euros into debt to attend college. Depending on family income, you can go to a great public university for €500-€4,000 per year (the latter = ~$4,665 right now).
So while you're busy being all relaxed about that and the fact that your President is not a monumental f*kwit, you're taking siesta during the day because you had a late one last night where you tied one on with friends (remember seeing them regularly?).
And nobody ever thought it was cool to brag that "dude, I'm an I-banker, I work 90 hours a week" like so many of the emotionally defenestrated droogs with whom I attended college. And, yeah, this is real, and so are they (sadly).
America, where mainstream culture says we must not be proud of having learned how to paraglide off a Swiss Alp (I actually did this...and definitely find it cooler than my income statement. Really wish I knew where the pics were to share--but I promise I shall find them!) or brew the perfect beer. But of how many GD hours we work. How much money we make. Those boys I went to college with? They for damn sure were proud of having memorized the Gordon Gekko speech from "Wall Street" ...
[](https://substack.com/redirect/526fd899-0ec3-473e-951c-09b79caaecfc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
> The point is, ladies and gentleman, that greed -- for lack of a better word -- is good.
> Greed is right.
> Greed works.
> Greed clarifies, cuts through, and captures the essence of the evolutionary spirit.
> Greed, in all of its forms -- greed for life, for money, for love, knowledge -- has marked the upward surge of mankind.
> And greed -- you mark my words -- will not only save Teldar Paper, but that other malfunctioning corporation called the USA.
Back in the Mediterranean region I just visited, when they're not finding that work life balance, they're taking advantage of a political culture that has ensured they have, at any new job: _a minimum of_ 20 days of vacation time from the start, _up to 10 months of partially paid parental leave._ It's almost as if they have time for-- and stick with me here my American friends--concepts known as "healing," "bonding with a newborn," "leisure time" and "rest."
No, these concepts weren't infected by a consumerist ethos brought to you by Reagan and reinforced by a monopoly Mitch McConnell, bought-and-paid for by creepy corporate creeps to steal as many judges--by any means necessary, how very Malcolm X of him--til they ensured this kinda of crazy talk would never take hold here in 'merica.
Our work til ya drop creed is backed up by billionaires robbing us in broad daylight, with the full support of its political wing--the GOP. A group of putzes who once paid a 91% top tax rate under Republican, Eisenhower, is now not so concerned about being squeezed for every last dollar or even many first ones.
Mrs. Jeff Bezos will have the funds for every last 3D printed body part, hell, she can go full cyborg if she wants! And while we're talking about Italy, [it's worth mentioning that incel-it-to-you-make-it Jeff Bezos rented out the island off Venice](https://substack.com/redirect/19a2fa47-a760-4d0f-8388-0a491b0d3c48?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--San Giorgio Maggiore--and spent a sickening $46-$56 million on it, while one in six American children--over 13 million--don't have enough _food_ to eat each day.
If I start, I won't stop with the expletives explaining how gauche, how utterly repulsive this is, so I'll just leave it at, any society allowing him to spend that on his wedding and taxing the living s*t out of him is failing. What I would have given to watch the crew from The Italian Job stick-up that squirrely, self-indulgent, self-congratulating, still-socially inept SOB.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/015eb7b1-f74e-4afc-b311-0d50a649c876?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The tragedy of this all is you didn't just imagine the 60s. America once tracked countries like Italy pretty closely with respect to our feelings on everything from work v. leisure to not allowing ping-pong-ball brains access to *all the guns.* But then 30 years of right-wing billionaire investment in changing our political culture with its media, think tanks, liars, scoundrels and thieves, paid off.
A B-list actor's smile and a lot of lies from a domestic psy-op that on close inspection looks a lot like things we did in places like Iran, Chile, Angola, Guatemala, Cuba. To put it another way: information warfare (or "disinformation").
The kind that helped convince the American public to allow this kindly-seeming old flibbertigibbet to engage in a pantsing of our welfare state, destructive deregulation and deliver a death blow to political and lifestyle views that had been as American as apple pie--or Italian and gelato--since FDR.
In other words, we once were found and then were lost.
And with that psy op more ruthless and effective than ever, we re-elected, Grover Cleveland style, a traitor, and convicted criminal, and adjudicated rapist, and lifetime conman, a guy with a jackknifed cerebral cortex and preference for Saddam Hussein chic in working and living quarters...with the piss cotton candy hair, ill-fitting suits and a new dollop of dementia every day.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8cf9cd55-f845-465a-9cc5-569224cd46eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Gaudy AF**
So, now Trump's right into the middle of the Rubicon. We haven't crossed it yet, as Julius Caesar did with troops to end the Roman republic. But we are definitely having a later-stage Roman Empire moment, as a palpable difference can just be felt in the air if one goes to a country like Italy now. People aren't worried about a masked Gestapo every bit as much as they're _not worried_ about having to work an 80-hour (and then bragging about it).
They're happy, healthy, enjoying their lives.
Guys and gals, we've been sold a con so big, even Bernie Madoff has to be sitting somewhere saying, "JFC, this is somer seriously f*ked up s*t." Italy is happy. We are not. Italy has a conservative leader who has promised to abide by the Italian Constitution, personally opposes gay civil unions (that's what they have there) and abortion but has promised to do nothing about it and is a staunch supporter of Ukraine.

* * *
None of these things can be said about our jack-in-the-box President, which is part of the reason we're so damn on edge these days. Or could it be that in a recent study, kids in the United States are **three times more likely to die** than kids in other wealthy countries? Let me repeat that--_kids_. You know, the "precious lives" conservatives claim to care so much about when they're busy forcing births and banning books.
But, once those kids are born? Good luck, junior. Donald Trump's creeping around your beauty pageants. You get gunned down in Newtown [or washed away in Texas](https://substack.com/redirect/9f5eb2ba-087c-4d65-8562-e6ea98970d8b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Hey, you're on your own, that's what we voted for.
Italian happiness vs our marked depression isn't a mystery. It's a choice. It's what happens when billionaires buy off Congress like it's a Black Friday sale. Rewire our system like Elon and his little helpers in the Treasury Department payment system. And treat working people like they're their first wives.
We've gutted public education, turned college into a luxury item, and take away people's retirements. Then we hand 'em a flag, a gun and a bootstrap speech and tell them to how America is the greatest country evah!
It's not. We're not. And this isn't normal, or an acceptable way to live. It's a rigged, soul-crushing, monopoly-led con game. Americans don't even realize how stressed they are--because if you weren't around before St. Ronnie, you may have never even had the luxury of feeling inner peace and personal security.
So yes, people in Italy were _much_ happier. They were _free_. Not in the fake "Don't tread on me" gun-humping way--but free to live, to breathe, to age, to vacation, to try a hobby, go out late with friends, raise kids without wondering if their next dental cleaning or ER visit will lead to homelessness. America still has some incredible people, resilient communities and deep wells of creativity.
But til we get the billionaire boots off our necks and build a government that once again serves _us_ --and we are currently headed in literally the opposite direction, led by President Numbnuts and rich [Heiling-Hitler](https://substack.com/redirect/32f5cd85-45b9-4e97-805e-a408d0308e93?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) guy who built the exploding ugly trucks. Hell, the entire oligarch class and their Congressional concubines.
And if we don't fix this kneecapped republic soon, well then I'm gonna need to keep taking European vacations to remember what dignity feels like. And think heart-attack seriously about living there again soon.
But perhaps, this time, on a permanent vacation.

### _**... Yo, Check This Out!**_
### Pam Bondi & the Epstein Files
* **Feb 27, 2025**
As **U.S. Attorney General** , Bondi announces the **first phase** of declassified Epstein documents--mostly previously leaked items--calling it "lifting the veil." She claimed the department initially handed over ~200 pages but that "thousands of pages" remain
* **Feb -Mar 2025**
Promises ratchet up as Bondi hints she **has a "list of Epstein's clients" sitting on her desk**, and that more revelations are imminent. Conservative voices rally behind her, expecting bombshell disclosures.
* **May 2025**
MAGA influencers receive bulky binders marked "Epstein Files" --but once opened, they're underwhelming: public info, redactions, nothing explosive [The Sun+10Vox+10The Independent+10](https://substack.com/redirect/5535d8cc-3906-49a8-b83a-06333d964d31?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Both sides start rolling their eyes.
* **July 7, 2025**
DOJ and the FBI release a memo stating **" no client list exists"**, no evidence Epstein blackmailed anyone, and that his death was suicide [Business Insider+13NPR+13Wikipedia+13](https://substack.com/redirect/93c475d3-93e2-48c4-8990-76b5e04af9cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Bondi's earlier claim about the client list is effectively debunked [ABC+13Wikipedia+13TIME+13](https://substack.com/redirect/d2d6b96a-01d1-46e4-a6e1-f582b32f574b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* **July 8 -9, 2025**
House Democrats (Jamie Raskin and others) demand **unredacted Epstein files mentioning Trump** from Bondi, accusing her of **sheltering Trump** [New York Post](https://substack.com/redirect/753ae1bf-0e3b-4d4f-9482-62d45253c99f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[The Wall Street Journal+2The Guardian+2The Daily Beast+2](https://substack.com/redirect/25b72d45-158e-4395-8de1-f8d28c86aaed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Meanwhile MAGA hardliners begin **blaming Bondi** , accusing her of political grandstanding [The Wall Street Journal](https://substack.com/redirect/abe49c5b-05bf-4707-94d4-e76117626f4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* **July 11 -12, 2025**
Heavy pressure mounts. Trump pleads: "let Pam Bondi do her job," urges MAGA faithful to stop the infighting [YouTube+5Forbes+5ABC+5](https://substack.com/redirect/7d4eae89-5670-445b-8c0d-79dcd3a1c76d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Bondi becomes the target instead of Trump.
* **July 13 -14, 2025**
**Justice Department doubles down** : no further documents will be released, citing court seals and victim privacy [New York Post+15AP News+15YouTube+15](https://substack.com/redirect/4b4768b8-4aca-494a-b98b-0219b4aaf12e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Conservative frustration boils over--**Bondi gets blamed** , not Trump, for the closure.

Situation, no motivation. Desperation, permanent vacation - Aerosmith, "Permanent Vacation" Jul 14 | | | | | | | | | | | THE BLUE LETTER #### Blue Amp is building something big..!! We 've watched for years as the Right built and exploited networked propaganda from The Heritage Foundation to FoxNews to your local Sinclair/Salem-owned tv & radio stations. We waited for billionaires to respond. But we 've grown tired of waiting as democracy's dying. So Blue Amp's building an ecosystem of hard-hitting creators, columnists & commentators. Amp donated five figures in support!--! We 're not billionaires.And without this ecosystem to counter RW conspiracies, lies & disinformation--365 days/yr--democracy won't survive. So with a Blue Amp! Thank you! 🔵 Subscribe now! Power indy media & oursave our democracy! The American Dream Is Now a European Vacation | | ---|---|--- LaGuardia Airport, July 13th As I sit here waiting for my connection to Cincy, the one that was supposed to leave from JFK but..cancelled. Then it was to slingshot me outta this other outer-borough airport by 8pm et...but creeps later and later into the evening with delays... As this has me wondering when I'll get home, I'm also really having a hard time answering another question that's on repeat--like a broken record or treasonous Trump acts--in my head. Why did I come back? Yeah, I know the obvious reasons. It's where my wife and I are raising our kids. We have older family members we wish to be here for. Etc. But the more existential one, I gotta tell you, I'm having some trouble with that. For the first time in a while, I'm seriously thinking about a not-too-distant future when I don't call our reality-bites politics and eternally exhausting, dog-squatting-by-a-fire-hydrant culture, home. Yes, I just flew in from 10 days in Rome and Sicily, and it's always hard to leave. Especially when it's these areas on the Mediterranean I've found myself returning to again and again (I lived in Nice, France, and have spent ~2 months of my life in and around Italy). But it still feels different this time. And it's not the pasta (ok, maybe a bit). Or the incredible wine (ok, maybe...). Or even the gorgeous Mediterranean sun and fantastically fun people I always meet. | | ---|---|--- It's that these fantastically fun people live in a country where hav ing a lif e is still a thing, and the government, both national and local, still function like they're supposed to: to serve the people. And this is Italy under Prime Minister Georgia Meloni, who runs one of the most conservative governments the country's had since World War II. Let's call to two items above personal culture and political culture. They interact to a great degree, as, for example, the control you exercise over your body will certainly going to influence your ability to enjoy passions and hobbies. But some of this is just a general belief system regarding what government should do and work/life balance. America is drastically failing on both of these, like Stephen Miller trying to keep his wife from pulling a "The Graduate" with Elon Musk. | | ---|---|--- Regular people in Italy aren't stressed, anxious and depressed like we are--especially our kids --for a variety of reasons. Nobody worries they won't be able to afford to treat an illness, because it just never would happen. Like every sane democracy, which is to say every single one but ours, healthcare is universal because OF COURSE IT IS. Like knowing Trump would be in Jeffrey Epstein's little black book. Universal, unless you're a MAGA birdbrain with a chronically malfunctioning sympathetic nervous system, such that fight or flight is set off by schools without Christmas trees or Superman movies. But back to our European friends, specifically in Rome and Sicily. They don't have to start saving for their children's college from their own first job post-puberty because it wouldn't cross their minds they'd fall hundreds of thousands of Euros into debt to attend college. Depending on family income, you can go to a great public university for €500-€4,000 per year (the latter = ~$4,665 right now). So while you're busy being all relaxed about that and the fact that your President is not a monumental fkwit, you're taking siesta during the day because you had a late one last night where you tied one on with friends (remember seeing them regularly?). And nobody ever thought it was cool to brag that "dude, I'm an I-banker, I work 90 hours a week" like so many of the emotionally defenestrated droogs with whom I attended college. And, yeah, this is real, and so are they (sadly). America, where mainstream culture says we must not be proud of having learned how to paraglide off a Swiss Alp (I actually did this...and definitely find it cooler than my income statement. Really wish I knew where the pics were to share--but I promise I shall find them!) or brew the perfect beer. But of how many GD hours we work. How much money we make. Those boys I went to college with? They for damn sure were proud of having memorized the Gordon Gekko speech from "Wall Street" ... The point is, ladies and gentleman, that greed -- for lack of a better word -- is good. Greed is right. Greed works. Greed clarifies, cuts through, and captures the essence of the evolutionary spirit. Greed, in all of its forms -- greed for life, for money, for love, knowledge -- has marked the upward surge of mankind. And greed -- you mark my words -- will not only save Teldar Paper, but that other malfunctioning corporation called the USA. Back in the Mediterranean region I just visited, when they're not finding that work life balance, they're taking advantage of a political culture that has ensured they have, at any new job: a minimum of 20 days of vacation time from the start, up to 10 months of partially paid parental leave. It's almost as if they have time for-- and stick with me here my American friends--concepts known as "healing," "bonding with a newborn," "leisure time" and "rest." No, these concepts weren't infected by a consumerist ethos brought to you by Reagan and reinforced by a monopoly Mitch McConnell, bought-and-paid for by creepy corporate creeps to steal as many judges--by any means necessary, how very Malcolm X of him--til they ensured this kinda of crazy talk would never take hold here in 'merica. Our work til ya drop creed is backed up by billionaires robbing us in broad daylight, with the full support of its political wing--the GOP. A group of putzes who once paid a 91% top tax rate under Republican, Eisenhower, is now not so concerned about being squeezed for every last dollar or even many first ones. Mrs. Jeff Bezos will have the funds for every last 3D printed body part, hell, she can go full cyborg if she wants! And while we're talking about Italy, Giorgio Maggiore--and spent a sickening $46-$56 million on it, while one in six American children--over 13 million--don't have enough food to eat each day. If I start, I won't stop with the expletives explaining how gauche, how utterly repulsive this is, so I'll just leave it at, any society allowing him to spend that on his wedding and taxing the living st out of him is failing. What I would have given to watch the crew from The Italian Job stick-up that squirrely, self-indulgent, self-congratulating, still-socially inept SOB. | | ---|---|--- The tragedy of this all is you didn't just imagine the 60s. America once tracked countries like Italy pretty closely with respect to our feelings on everything from work v. leisure to not allowing ping-pong-ball brains access to all the guns. But then 30 years of right-wing billionaire investment in changing our political culture with its media, think tanks, liars, scoundrels and thieves, paid off. A B-list actor's smile and a lot of lies from a domestic psy-op that on close inspection looks a lot like things we did in places like Iran, Chile, Angola, Guatemala, Cuba. To put it another way: information warfare (or "disinformation"). The kind that helped convince the American public to allow this kindly-seeming old flibbertigibbet to engage in a pantsing of our welfare state, destructive deregulation and deliver a death blow to political and lifestyle views that had been as American as apple pie--or Italian and gelato--since FDR. In other words, we once were found and then were lost. And with that psy op more ruthless and effective than ever, we re-elected, Grover Cleveland style, a traitor, and convicted criminal, and adjudicated rapist, and lifetime conman, a guy with a jackknifed cerebral cortex and preference for Saddam Hussein chic in working and living quarters...with the piss cotton candy hair, ill-fitting suits and a new dollop of dementia every day. | | ---|---|--- Gaudy AF So, now Trump's right into the middle of the Rubicon. We haven't crossed it yet, as Julius Caesar did with troops to end the Roman republic. But we are definitely having a later-stage Roman Empire moment, as a palpable difference can just be felt in the air if one goes to a country like Italy now. People aren't worried about a masked Gestapo every bit as much as they're not worried about having to work an 80-hour (and then bragging about it). They're happy, healthy, enjoying their lives. Guys and gals, we've been sold a con so big, even Bernie Madoff has to be sitting somewhere saying, "JFC, this is somer seriously fked up st." Italy is happy. We are not. Italy has a conservative leader who has promised to abide by the Italian Constitution, personally opposes gay civil unions (that's what they have there) and abortion but has promised to do nothing about it and is a staunch supporter of Ukraine. `Full Blue Amp Subscription For Just .26/Day!` Get the TRUTH corporate media hides, from a former Biden ad man & former Emmy-winning MSNBC anchor who know the stories behind the stories. And Amplify Good. . Save Democracy! None of these things can be said about our jack-in-the-box President, which is part of the reason we're so damn on edge these days. Or could it be that in a recent study, kids in the United States are three times more likely to die than kids in other wealthy countries? Let me repeat that--kids. You know, the "precious lives" conservatives claim to care so much about when they're busy forcing births and banning books. But, once those kids are born? Good luck, junior. Donald Trump's creeping around your beauty pageants. You get gunned down in Newtown . Hey, you're on your own, that's what we voted for. Italian happiness vs our marked depression isn't a mystery. It's a choice. It's what happens when billionaires buy off Congress like it's a Black Friday sale. Rewire our system like Elon and his little helpers in the Treasury Department payment system. And treat working people like they're their first wives. We've gutted public education, turned college into a luxury item, and take away people's retirements. Then we hand 'em a flag, a gun and a bootstrap speech and tell them to how America is the greatest country evah! It's not. We're not. And this isn't normal, or an acceptable way to live. It's a rigged, soul-crushing, monopoly-led con game. Americans don't even realize how stressed they are--because if you weren't around before St. Ronnie, you may have never even had the luxury of feeling inner peace and personal security. So yes, people in Italy were much happier. They were free. Not in the fake "Don't tread on me" gun-humping way--but free to live, to breathe, to age, to vacation, to try a hobby, go out late with friends, raise kids without wondering if their next dental cleaning or ER visit will lead to homelessness. America still has some incredible people, resilient communities and deep wells of creativity. But til we get the billionaire boots off our necks and build a government that once again serves us --and we are currently headed in literally the opposite direction, led by President Numbnuts and rich guy who built the exploding ugly trucks. Hell, the entire oligarch class and their Congressional concubines. And if we don't fix this kneecapped republic soon, well then I'm gonna need to keep taking European vacations to remember what dignity feels like. And think heart-attack seriously about living there again soon. But perhaps, this time, on a permanent vacation. ... Yo, Check This Out! Pam Bondi & the Epstein Files Feb 27, 2025 As U.S. Attorney General , Bondi announces the first phase of declassified Epstein documents--mostly previously leaked items--calling it "lifting the veil." She claimed the department initially handed over ~200 pages but that "thousands of pages" remain Feb -Mar 2025 Promises ratchet up as Bondi hints she has a "list of Epstein's clients" sitting on her desk, and that more revelations are imminent. Conservative voices rally behind her, expecting bombshell disclosures. May 2025 MAGA influencers receive bulky binders marked "Epstein Files" --but once opened, they're underwhelming: public info, redactions, nothing explosive . Both sides start rolling their eyes. July 7, 2025 DOJ and the FBI release a memo stating " no client list exists", no evidence Epstein blackmailed anyone, and that his death was suicide . Bondi's earlier claim about the client list is effectively debunked . July 8 -9, 2025 House Democrats (Jamie Raskin and others) demand unredacted Epstein files mentioning Trump from Bondi, accusing her of sheltering Trump . Meanwhile MAGA hardliners begin blaming Bondi , accusing her of political grandstanding . July 11 -12, 2025 Heavy pressure mounts. Trump pleads: "let Pam Bondi do her job," urges MAGA faithful to stop the infighting . Bondi becomes the target instead of Trump. July 13 -14, 2025 Justice Department doubles down : no further documents will be released, citing court seals and victim privacy . Conservative frustration boils over--Bondi gets blamed , not Trump, for the closure. 🐱 KITTY PIC! name: Team Jon Ossoff
subject: “TOSS-UP”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

Peter,
We’re asking you to start your week by [**making a contribution to Jon’s re-election campaign. Here’s why:**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-july25.vLE_dc&refcode=em250714-1-f&t=1&akid=549%2E5691347%2E8WqX38)
* _Cook Political Report_ has officially called this race a “TOSS-UP” and said Jon Ossoff is the “most endangered incumbent” in the country.

* _The Hill_ reported “Sen. Jon Ossoff (Ga.) is considered the most vulnerable Democrat on the Senate map, and Republicans have been eagerly awaiting the chance to win back the seat.”

* And _NBC News_ has also called Jon “Democrats’ most vulnerable Senator.”

Peter, Republicans know that if they win Georgia, Democrats will have almost no chance of winning back the Senate.
That’s why GOP-aligned Super PACs are ALREADY spending millions of dollars attacking Jon. _And it’s why we need to be fighting back every step of the way._
So we do have to ask for your support. But it's important to Jon, especially right now, that people are contributing _only_ if they are in a position to do so, and _only_ what they can afford.
If it's $25, great. If it's $1, that will help too and means a lot because it shows you're in the fight. And if you can't donate today, that's fine too. But with right-wing attacks already coming Jon’s way, we have to fight back:
[**Can Jon count on you to make a contribution — $5, $25 or whatever you can afford — to his campaign today? We will put it to work fighting back against MAGA extremists and protecting this seat.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-july25.vLE_dc&refcode=em250714-1-f&t=2&akid=549%2E5691347%2E8WqX38)
**[DONATE NOW](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-july25.vLE_dc&refcode=em250714-1-f&t=3&akid=549%2E5691347%2E8WqX38)**
Anything you can give would mean a lot to our campaign.
Thank you,
Jon Ossoff for Senate

Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Thank you for supporting Jon Ossoff. Email is a critical way to keep up with Jon and his work in the US Senate, but you can click [here](https://act.electjon.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=549%2E5691347%2E8WqX38) if you'd like to unsubscribe.

name: Patty Murray
subject: the real reason why the GOP is canceling PBS and NPR
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PEOPLE FOR PATTY MURRAY
body:
(Spoiler: it's to help offset the deficit-wrecking cost of passing trillions in tax breaks for billionaires and big corporations.)
| | |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111473191/557377951/-145648726?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk523-fr-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOTQyMmExZi0wMjYxLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Qst6Oe6_7ZuQpmQ6eVkpsaC5_z-Z-sPqpIn47IbdxnE=)
That's right, Peter.
According to Trump and House Republicans, time-honored, award-winning, and widely beloved programming like:
* _PBS NewsHour_
* _All Things Considered_
* _Sesame Street_
* _Morning Edition_
* _Frontline_
* _Wait, Wait... Don't Tell Me!_
* _Antiques Roadshow_
* _Fresh Air_
* _NOVA_
* _Mr. Rogers_
* _American Experience_

**– and countless other productions broadcast by local PBS and NPR stations nationwide now count as "radical, woke propaganda."**
Maybe it's because, despite the fact that taxpayer dollars only make up a small amount of their annual budgets, they're 100% free for all Americans to enjoy.
Maybe it's because even the remotest rural communities can tune in – so that lifesaving emergency alerts can reach the folks who most urgently need to hear them.
Maybe it's because they air educational, nuanced, and thought-provoking programs for viewers of all ages and backgrounds.
Maybe it's because their independent journalism isn't afraid to fact-check Trump's lies and expose the harm of his extremist policies.
Or maybe Republicans think that cutting PBS and getting our kids hooked on brainrot TV is actually a win.

After all, if our kids watch PBS, they could learn to count!

And if our kids learn to count, how will the GOP ever convince anyone that trillions of dollars in tax giveaways for billionaires are free?
Whatever the bogus reason Republicans are cooking up for these cuts, recent data shows that Americans support their tax dollars funding NPR and PBS _by a two-to-one margin,_ I will do everything I can to ensure they stay on the air, no matter how Trump goes after them next.
It starts by blocking House Republicans' bill once it reaches the Senate – and that could happen at any time in the next 48 hours.

Since the deck is still stacked in Trump's favor,**our fundraising strength is one of our best hopes of proving to Republicans that if they keep selling us out to fuel his unpopular, out-of-touch, and anti-working family policies, they'll have _ _ to pay a steep electoral price come next Election Day.**
**[So will you please chip in $5, or whatever makes the most sense for you, to help us make some noise before this bill comes to the Senate for a vote?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111473192/557377952/-145648726?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk523-fr-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOTQyMmExZi0wMjYxLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Qst6Oe6_7ZuQpmQ6eVkpsaC5_z-Z-sPqpIn47IbdxnE=)**

name: In This Together PAC
subject: Andy stood up, the GOP refused to listen, and now Americans are fighting back.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by In This Together PAC
body:
Peter,

**FIRST:** _Gov. Andy Beshear stood up for the people of Kentucky and rural America._ He went on national news stations, calling out the GOP’s reckless tax bill — warning that it would devastate families in Kentucky and hurt working people nationwide.

**THEN:** _Republicans didn’t listen._ They rammed through a bill that guts almost a trillion dollars from Medicaid, demolishes SNAP benefits, and will close rural hospitals and balloon the national debt by trillions—all while giving tax breaks to the ultra-wealthy.

**NOW:** _It’s on us._ We have to hold these so-called "leaders" accountable at the ballot box.

Peter, In This Together PAC was built to support candidates who fight for their communities, listen to their constituents, and vote to help the people they represent, not hurt them.

It’s clear our current leaders aren’t doing that, so we’re fighting back. But if we’re going to support good candidates in tough races, we need your help before tomorrow’s mid-month deadline at midnight.

[**Will you chip in right now to help us hit our goal and help us elect for-the-people leaders who will put their constituents over special interests? name: AOC
subject: Unpredictable things can happen.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
There is no action too small.
#
Please do not fall for the illusion or the lie that any action is too small. **There is no action too small**.
In fact, when millions of us _do_ tiny things, we _can_ disrupt these systems. I'm not saying that getting in the game is going 100% to change the outcome, but it may, because **we are in an unprecedented time**.
The unprecedented time can mean, of course, many bad things happen — but it can also mean that **unpredictable things can happen, and can even go our way**.
I was elected in the first Trump administration and nobody ever, _ever_ thought that I had a prayer in order to win. I was a waitress. I was challenging the head of a political machine who was supposed to be the next Speaker of the House.
They spent $3 million against me while I was wiping down tables, and we won. Why?
**Because an unpredictable window has opened**.
Do not comply in advance. You try anyway.
We will not be afraid – **because that’s what they want**.
But our movement for real progress will have to push harder than ever these next four years.
[Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250714.hOHyQf&refcode=em20250714-9316-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9316%2E2526796%2ERdEzqe)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250714.hOHyQf&refcode=em20250714-9316-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=9316%2E2526796%2ERdEzqe)**
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20250714?refcode=em20250714-9316-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=21&refcode2=9316_2526796_RdEzqe&akid=9316%2E2526796%2ERdEzqe)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20250714-9316-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=22&akid=9316%2E2526796%2ERdEzqe)**
